items.situation: a student asks an advising-office staff member which elective to register for next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to pick an elective for next term, and I'm stuck between Intro to Film and Public Speaking.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Both are popular. What's your major again?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Marketing. I figured Public Speaking might be more useful, but it meets at eight in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, the system shows Public Speaking still has a few open seats. Film's actually waitlisted already.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, waitlisted? Then I guess the choice kind of makes itself.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Pretty much. And honestly, presentation skills will help you a lot in marketing.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about the Intro to Film course?

options.A: It already has a waitlist.
options.B: It meets early in the morning.
options.C: It is not useful for marketing.
options.D: It has several open seats left.

questions.explanation: 男生明确说“Film's actually waitlisted already”，所以电影课已经有等候名单，A 正确。B 把“早上八点上课”这个细节张冠李戴了——那是女生说的 Public Speaking 的情况；C 男生说的是 Public Speaking 对市场营销有帮助，并没说电影课没用；D 与对话相反，有空位的是 Public Speaking，不是电影课。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Drop the elective requirement entirely
options.B: Add her name to the film course waitlist
options.C: Register for the public speaking course
options.D: Switch her major to communications

questions.explanation: 电影课已满且需排队，而 Public Speaking 还有空位、又对市场营销有用，女生也说“the choice kind of makes itself”，因此她最可能报名 Public Speaking，C 正确。A 属于过度推断，她从未想放弃选修；B 是她实际上要避开的选项（排队等候）；D 完全没有依据，对话从未提到换专业。

items.situation: a student asks a bookstore clerk whether to buy a course textbook now or wait for the rental restock

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Excuse me, do you still have the biology textbook for sale? The shelf looks empty.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We sold out of the new copies this morning, but the rental version comes back in stock Friday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Friday? My first lecture is Thursday, though.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The first chapter's posted free online, so you'd be fine for one class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that helps. Is the rental a lot cheaper?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: About half the price. I'd just wait the two days if I were you.

questions.stem: Why is the textbook currently unavailable?

options.A: The store never ordered enough copies.
options.B: The new copies sold out that morning.
options.C: The publisher delayed the latest edition.
options.D: It can only be purchased online now.

questions.explanation: 店员说“We sold out of the new copies this morning”，所以是当天上午新书售罄，B 正确。A 没有依据，店员没说订得不够；C 对话从未提到出版社延迟；D 把“第一章免费在线”误读成整本书只能网购，店员说的是周五租赁版会补货。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Buy a new copy from another bookstore
options.B: Drop the biology course this term
options.C: Skip her Thursday lecture entirely
options.D: Wait until Friday for the rental copy

questions.explanation: 第一章免费在线让她周四的课不受影响，租赁版又便宜一半且周五到货，店员也建议“just wait the two days”，所以她最可能等到周五租书，D 正确。A 没有依据，没提到去别家书店；B 属于过度推断，她从未想退课；C 与对话相反——免费章节正是让她不必缺课。

items.situation: a student talks with a transportation-office clerk about getting a bus pass, a bike permit, or just walking

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just moved into the north dorms and I'm trying to figure out the cheapest way to get to class.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. We offer a semester bus pass, a bike permit, or, if you're close, walking's free of course.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The north dorms are about a twenty-five minute walk, which feels like a lot before an early class.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then the bus pass is probably your best bet. The route from north dorms runs every ten minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: And the bike permit? I do have a bike back home I could bring up.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's cheaper than the bus, but with winter coming, most students up north end up regretting it.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to register a bicycle on campus
options.B: Which dormitory the woman should choose
options.C: The best commuting option for the woman
options.D: Why the campus bus route was changed

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女生“最便宜/最合适的到课方式”——公交、自行车还是步行——展开，因此主旨是为她选择通勤方式，C 正确。A 只是其中一个被讨论的选项（自行车许可），太窄；B 她已经搬进北宿舍，并不在选宿舍；D 对话从未提到线路变更。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about getting the bike permit?

options.A: It may be a poor choice once winter arrives
options.B: It is currently sold out for the semester
options.C: It costs more than the semester bus pass
options.D: It is not available to north dorm residents

questions.explanation: 男生说自行车许可虽更便宜，但“with winter coming, most students up north end up regretting it”，暗示入冬后骑车不是好选择，A 正确。B 没有依据，他没说许可售罄；C 与对话相反，他明确说自行车比公交便宜；D 也不成立，他并未说北宿舍居民不能办许可。

items.situation: a student asks a library staff member about a novel that is checked out, exploring why it is in such high demand

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm looking for the novel Northern Light, but the catalog says all copies are checked out.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ah, that one. A professor put it on the required reading list for two big literature sections this term.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that explains the wait. So it's not just me who couldn't find it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. We've actually ordered extra copies, but they won't arrive for a couple of weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I just need it for one essay, not the whole term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: In that case, the e-book version has no waitlist at all. You can borrow it instantly.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: A complaint about an inaccurate library catalog
options.B: A disagreement over a professor's reading list
options.C: Why the library is purchasing fewer novels
options.D: A student's difficulty obtaining a popular novel

questions.explanation: 对话核心是女生想借却借不到这本热门小说，馆员解释原因并给出替代方案，因此主旨是她获取这本小说遇到困难，D 正确。A 目录其实是准确的（确实都被借走了），并非投诉目录有误；B 没人对阅读书单有异议；C 与对话相反，图书馆是加订而非减少。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Wait two weeks for the new print copies
options.B: Borrow the e-book version of the novel
options.C: Add her name to the physical hold list
options.D: Ask the professor to lend her a copy

questions.explanation: 女生只为一篇论文短期需要这本书，而电子版“no waitlist at all, borrow it instantly”，因此她最可能借电子版，B 正确。A 是被排除的选项——她明说不需要等整个学期；C 纸质版仍要排队，与她“只用一篇论文”的需求不符，且馆员已提供更快的电子版；D 完全没有依据，对话没提向教授借书。

items.situation: a student asks a dining-services staff member about a meal-plan swipe problem before grabbing lunch

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my meal card just got declined at the register, but I'm sure I still have swipes left.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check. Hmm, the system shows your plan reset this morning, so your swipes are actually fine.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then why won't it scan?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Looks like the magnetic strip on your card is worn out. The reader just can't read it anymore.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. Can I still get lunch right now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. I'll enter your ID number manually today, and you can grab a free replacement card at the ID office.

questions.stem: Why was the woman's meal card declined?

options.A: The card's magnetic strip is worn out.
options.B: Her meal plan ran out of swipes.
options.C: Her plan had not reset for the term.
options.D: She typed in the wrong ID number.

questions.explanation: 店员检查后说“the magnetic strip on your card is worn out. The reader just can't read it anymore”，所以是磁条磨损导致刷不出，A 正确。B 与对话相反，系统显示她的次数其实够用；C 也相反，店员说她的套餐当天早上已经重置；D 没有依据，手动输 ID 是店员之后才做的事，不是被拒原因。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after lunch?

options.A: Add more swipes to her meal plan
options.B: Pay for her lunch with cash instead
options.C: Get a replacement card at the ID office
options.D: Report the broken register to a manager

questions.explanation: 店员明确说她可以去 ID 办公室“grab a free replacement card”，磁条坏了也确实需要换卡，因此她午饭后最可能去办新卡，C 正确。A 没有依据，她的次数本来就够，不需要加；B 是被排除的——店员手动输入 ID 让她不必付现；D 问题出在卡的磁条而非收银机，所以无需报修收银机。

items.situation: a student asks the registrar staff to fix a closed class and gets onto a waitlist

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to register for Statistics 201, but the system keeps saying the section is closed. Is there any way to get in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check. Yeah, that morning section filled up last week. But there's a waitlist, and honestly, spots open up pretty often in the first two weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? So I should just add my name?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. You sign up through the student portal, not here. If a seat opens, you'll get an email and have twenty-four hours to claim it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, twenty-four hours. I'll keep an eye on my inbox then. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the registrar's office?

options.A: To drop a statistics course she dislikes
options.B: To get help registering for a full class
options.C: To complain about a billing error
options.D: To change her major to statistics

questions.explanation: 女士开口就说她想注册Statistics 201但系统显示该section已关闭，问有没有办法进去，所以她是来寻求帮助注册一门已满的课。A错误，她想加入而非退课，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。C错误，对话完全没提到账单问题，是另一个校园话题（wrong_topic）。D错误，她只是想注册一门统计课，并未提及换专业（wrong_topic）。

questions.stem: What does the man say will happen if a seat opens up?

options.A: She will be charged a waitlist fee
options.B: The office will call her phone
options.C: She will receive an email and have 24 hours to claim it
options.D: She must come back to the office in person

questions.explanation: 男士明确说如果有座位空出，她会收到一封邮件，并有二十四小时来确认。C正确。A错误，对话从未提到任何候补费用（related_but_not_stated）。B错误，男士说的是邮件通知，不是打电话，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。D错误，男士特意说候补名单要通过学生门户网站操作，而不是来办公室，与原文相反（misattributed）。

items.situation: a student asks library staff about an overdue book and renewal options

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I think this book is overdue. I totally forgot about it. How much do I owe?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Let me scan it. Hmm, it's two days late, but there's no fine. We dropped late fees on regular loans last semester.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's a relief! I actually still need it for my paper. Can I keep it longer?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure, I can renew it for another two weeks, as long as nobody else has placed a hold on it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great, nobody has, right? Then please renew it. Thanks so much!

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man about the overdue book?

options.A: There is no late fee to pay
options.B: He owes a small fine for two days
options.C: The book must be returned today
options.D: He is banned from borrowing again

questions.explanation: 女士扫描后说虽然晚了两天，但没有罚款，因为上学期取消了常规借阅的滞纳金。A正确。B错误，她明确说没有罚款，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。C错误，她反而同意续借两周，并未要求今天归还，与原文相反（misattributed）。D错误，对话从未提到禁止借书（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Charge the man a renewal fee
options.B: Renew the book for two more weeks
options.C: Place a hold on the book for someone else
options.D: Tell the man to find a different copy

questions.explanation: 男士确认没人预约后请女士续借，女士之前也说只要没人预约就可以续借两周，所以她接下来最可能续借这本书。B正确。A错误，续借没有提到任何费用，属于过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，给别人下预约与对话情境相反，是被否定的情况（rejected_option）。D错误，让他另找一本与对话内容无关（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff to switch to a quieter dorm room

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to request a room change. My current room faces the courtyard, and there's noise from events almost every night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm sorry to hear that. We do have a few openings on the quiet floor in Building C. It's reserved for students who need a low-noise environment.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds perfect. Is there a fee to transfer?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No fee, but you'll need to fill out a transfer form and have your current roommate sign off that you've cleared your things.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. Where do I get the form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the housing portal under 'Room Transfer.' Submit it online, and we'll process it within three business days.

questions.stem: What does the man say the woman must do to transfer?

options.A: Pay a small transfer fee first
options.B: Find a replacement for her old room
options.C: Submit a form with her roommate's sign-off
options.D: Move out within twenty-four hours

questions.explanation: 男士说没有费用，但她需要填一份转宿表，并让现在的室友签字确认她已清空物品，所以正确答案是提交带室友签字的表格。C正确。A错误，男士明确说没有费用，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B错误，对话从未提到要为旧房间找接替者（related_but_not_stated）。D错误，男士说处理需要三个工作日，并未要求二十四小时内搬出（wrong_detail）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Move into Building C right away
options.B: Go online to find and submit the transfer form
options.C: Ask her roommate to switch rooms too
options.D: Complain to the event organizers about the noise

questions.explanation: 男士告诉她表格在住宿门户网站的'Room Transfer'栏目下，在线提交即可，所以她接下来最可能上网找到并提交转宿表。B正确。A错误，转宿需要先提交表格并等三个工作日处理，立刻搬入属于过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，对话只需室友签字确认，没说室友也要换房，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。D错误，她选择的是换房而不是向活动组织者投诉噪音，是被放弃的做法（rejected_option）。

items.situation: a student asks IT staff why campus Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting on her laptop

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my laptop keeps dropping off the campus Wi-Fi every few minutes. It's driving me crazy during lectures.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Are you connected to the network called 'CampusNet' or the one labeled 'CampusNet-Guest'?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Um, the guest one, I think. Does that matter?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the issue. The guest network times out every fifteen minutes by design. You should register your laptop on the secured 'CampusNet' instead, and it'll stay connected.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh! How do I register it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Forget the guest network first, then select 'CampusNet' and log in with your student ID and password. It remembers your device after that.

questions.stem: According to the man, why does the woman's laptop keep disconnecting?

options.A: Her laptop's wireless card is faulty
options.B: She is on the guest network, which times out
options.C: The campus Wi-Fi is down for maintenance
options.D: Her student ID has expired

questions.explanation: 男士指出问题在于她连的是访客网络'CampusNet-Guest'，而该网络按设计每十五分钟超时一次，所以掉线是因为用了会超时的访客网络。B正确。A错误，男士没说她的无线网卡有问题，原因是网络选错了（wrong_detail）。C错误，对话没提到Wi-Fi在维护停机（wrong_topic）。D错误，学生ID会用来登录安全网络，但从未说它过期了（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a new wireless adapter for her laptop
options.B: Keep using the guest network during lectures
options.C: Forget the guest network and log in to CampusNet
options.D: Report the problem to her professor

questions.explanation: 男士告诉她先忘记访客网络，再选'CampusNet'并用学生ID和密码登录，设备就会被记住，所以她接下来最可能这样做。C正确。A错误，问题不在硬件，买新网卡属于过度推断（over_inference）。B错误，继续用会超时的访客网络与建议相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，向教授报告这个技术问题与对话内容无关（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks dining-hall staff about adding more money to a meal plan

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, my meal plan balance is almost empty, and it's only mid-semester. Can I add more money to it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure! You can add 'dining dollars' anytime. A lot of students top up around now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh good. Do I have to pay cash here at the counter?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No, it's easier online. Just log into the dining account page and add funds with a card. It shows up on your ID instantly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect, instant is great. I'll do it before lunch then. Thanks!

questions.stem: How does the woman say the man can add money to his meal plan?

options.A: By paying cash at the dining counter
options.B: By visiting the registrar's office
options.C: By adding funds online with a card
options.D: By waiting until the next semester

questions.explanation: 女士说不用付现金，在线更方便，登录餐饮账户页面用银行卡充值即可，且会立刻显示在ID上，所以正确答案是在线用卡充值。C正确。A错误，她明确说不用在柜台付现金，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B错误，对话没提到去注册办公室，属另一个校园场所（wrong_topic）。D错误，她说随时都能充值，男士也打算午餐前就充，无需等到下学期（wrong_detail）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Add dining dollars online before lunch
options.B: Pay cash at the counter right now
options.C: Cancel his meal plan for the semester
options.D: Ask his roommate to share a meal plan

questions.explanation: 男士听完后说'我午餐前就去弄'，而女士建议的方式是在线充值，所以他接下来最可能在午餐前在线充入餐饮美元。A正确。B错误，女士说不用付现金，男士也选了在线方式，现金支付是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，他想充钱继续用，取消餐饮计划与情境相反，属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到和室友共用餐饮计划，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks an academic advisor whether to drop a heavy course load

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm taking six courses this term, and I'm completely overwhelmed. I'm thinking of dropping one, but I'm worried about falling behind.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six is a lot. Which one are you considering dropping?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Probably the elective, the art history one. It's interesting, but it's the heaviest reading load.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Here's the thing: dropping an elective won't delay your graduation at all, since you've already met your core requirements for the year. You can always retake it later.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's reassuring. So it won't hurt me down the line?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. And the drop deadline is Friday, so you have time to think it over before deciding.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main concern in this conversation?

options.A: She failed an art history exam
options.B: She wants to add a seventh course
options.C: Whether dropping a course will delay her graduation
options.D: She cannot find the art history classroom

questions.explanation: 女士说她修了六门课感到不堪重负，想退掉一门但担心落后、影响进度，顾问也正是围绕'退课不会推迟毕业'来安慰她，所以她主要担心退课是否会推迟毕业。C正确。A错误，对话没提到考试不及格（wrong_topic）。B错误，她想退掉一门而不是再加一门，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。D错误，对话从未涉及找不到教室（wrong_topic）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Drop the art history course immediately
options.B: Take time to decide before Friday's deadline
options.C: Add another elective to her schedule
options.D: Switch her major to art history

questions.explanation: 顾问最后说退课截止日是周五，她还有时间再考虑后再决定，女士也表现出宽慰但未当场决定，所以她最可能在周五截止前花点时间考虑。B正确。A错误，顾问特意说她有时间考虑，立刻退课属过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，她正为课业过重发愁，再加一门选修与情境相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，对话没提到换专业，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks bookstore staff whether to buy or rent a pricey textbook

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Excuse me, this chemistry textbook is almost two hundred dollars. Is there a cheaper option?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You can rent it for the semester for about a third of that price. Most students go that route.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A third? That's a huge difference. Are there any downsides to renting?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Just that you have to return it by the last day of finals, and you can't write in it. If you like keeping your books, buying makes more sense.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, I never reread my chem books anyway. Renting it is.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good choice. Bring it to the counter and I'll set up the rental for you.

questions.stem: What is one downside of renting the textbook, according to the woman?

options.A: It costs more than buying it new
options.B: It cannot be used during finals
options.C: You must return it and cannot write in it
options.D: It is only available as an e-book

questions.explanation: 女士说租书的缺点是必须在期末考试最后一天前归还，而且不能在书上做笔记，所以正确答案是要归还且不能写字。C正确。A错误，租书只要约三分之一的价格，比买便宜，与原文相反（misattributed）。B错误，她说的是期末最后一天前归还，并非期末期间不能用，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到电子书（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Take the book to the counter to rent it
options.B: Buy the textbook at full price
options.C: Look for the book at another store
options.D: Borrow the textbook from a classmate

questions.explanation: 男士说自己反正从不重读化学书，决定租书，女士也让他把书拿到柜台办理租赁，所以他接下来最可能把书拿到柜台租。A正确。B错误，他已决定租而非买，全价购买是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，对话没提到去别的店找，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。D错误，向同学借书也未被提及，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks gym staff how to sign up for a fitness class that looks full

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I want to join the Tuesday spin class, but the sign-up sheet looks full. Is it too late?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The paper sheet is just a backup. We actually take sign-ups through the rec center app now, and a few spots are still open.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't know there was an app. Is it free to join the class?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Totally free with your student membership. Just download the app, search 'Tuesday Spin,' and tap reserve.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Easy enough. I'll grab the app right now. Thanks!

questions.stem: How does the man say the woman should sign up for the spin class?

options.A: By writing her name on the paper sheet
options.B: By paying a class fee at the desk
options.C: Through the rec center app
options.D: By emailing the class instructor

questions.explanation: 男士说纸质签到表只是备用，现在都是通过rec center应用报名，而且还有空位，所以正确答案是通过健身中心的应用报名。C正确。A错误，他说纸质表只是备用，真正报名要用应用，改了关键方式（wrong_detail）。B错误，他说凭学生会员完全免费，并非在前台交费（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到给教练发邮件（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Download the app and reserve a spot
options.B: Add her name to the paper sign-up sheet
options.C: Pay for a gym membership upgrade
options.D: Try a different class on another day

questions.explanation: 女士说'我现在就去下载应用'，而男士的指引是下载应用、搜索'Tuesday Spin'并点预约，所以她接下来最可能下载应用并预约一个名额。A正确。B错误，纸质表只是备用，写名字是被实际放弃的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，课程凭学生会员免费，升级会员属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到换别的日子上别的课，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks health-center staff how to book a flu shot appointment

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to get a flu shot. Do I need an appointment, or can I just walk in?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: For flu shots this week, we're doing walk-ins only, no appointment needed. But the wait can be long in the afternoons.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good to know. So mornings are quieter?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Much quieter, right when we open at nine. Also, bring your insurance card, though the shot itself is free for students.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Free is great. I have a class until ten-thirty, so I'll come right after. See you then.

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man about getting a flu shot this week?

options.A: He must book an appointment in advance
options.B: It is walk-in only, with no appointment needed
options.C: Flu shots are unavailable until next week
options.D: He needs a referral from a doctor first

questions.explanation: 女士说本周流感疫苗只接受walk-in、不需要预约，所以正确答案是无需预约、直接来打。B正确。A错误，她明确说不用预约，与原文相反（misattributed）。C错误，她说本周就在打疫苗，并非下周才有（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到需要医生转诊（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Come in for a walk-in shot after his 10:30 class
options.B: Book an afternoon appointment online
options.C: Wait until next week to get the shot
options.D: Ask his doctor for a flu shot instead

questions.explanation: 男士说他有课到十点半，打算下课后马上过来，而女士说上午刚开门时人少且接受walk-in，所以他最可能在十点半下课后过来walk-in打针。A正确。B错误，本周是walk-in、不用预约，且下午人多，预约下午与建议相反，属被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，他打算今天就来，等到下周属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到去找自己的医生打，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff why a scholarship payment hasn't arrived

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my scholarship was supposed to be applied to my tuition this month, but my account still shows a balance due. Did something go wrong?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me pull up your file. Ah, I see. Your scholarship is approved, but it's on hold because one document is still missing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Missing? I thought I submitted everything.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You're missing the signed enrollment verification. Once we receive that, the funds release within about two business days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, where do I find that form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the financial-aid portal under 'Forms.' Sign it electronically and upload it, and you'll be all set.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the financial-aid office?

options.A: To apply for a brand-new scholarship
options.B: To find out why her scholarship hasn't paid her tuition
options.C: To appeal a rejected scholarship application
options.D: To request a refund of overpaid tuition

questions.explanation: 女士一开口就说奖学金本应这个月抵学费，但账户仍显示有欠款，问是不是出了问题，所以她是来弄清楚奖学金为何还没抵付学费。B正确。A错误，她已有获批的奖学金，不是来申请全新的（wrong_topic）。C错误，男士说奖学金是approved，并未被拒，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。D错误，她账户是欠款而非多付，要求退款与情境相反（reversed_situation）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Sign and upload the verification form online
options.B: Pay the tuition balance herself today
options.C: Reapply for the scholarship from scratch
options.D: Wait two business days and do nothing

questions.explanation: 男士说缺的是签好的enrollment verification表，并指出它在财务资助门户的'Forms'栏目下，电子签名后上传即可，所以她接下来最可能在线签署并上传该表格。A正确。B错误，问题在于奖学金被暂缓而非真欠款，自己掏钱付学费属过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，奖学金已获批，重新申请与情境相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，资金需要她先交表才会发放，干等什么都不做与男士的指引相反（rejected_option）。

items.situation: a student asks registrar staff to correct a misspelled name on official records before transcripts go out

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just noticed my last name is misspelled on my student record. It says 'Harten' but it should be 'Hartman.' Can you fix that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, we definitely want that corrected before transcripts go out. Do you have a photo ID with the correct spelling?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, here's my driver's license.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll update it in the system right now. It'll take about two business days to fully process across all your records.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Two days? My transcript request is going out this Friday. Will the new spelling show up in time?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It should, but to be safe, I'd hold off on submitting that transcript request until the correction clears. Just come back Thursday and we'll confirm it's done.

questions.stem: What does the man ask the woman to provide?

options.A: A photo ID with the correct spelling
options.B: A copy of her birth certificate
options.C: Her most recent transcript
options.D: A signed correction form

questions.explanation: 男士明确问女士是否有拼写正确的带照片证件（photo ID），女士随后出示了驾照，所以A正确。B出生证明从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。C成绩单是女士要寄出的东西，不是男士索要的证件，属于misattributed。D签字的更正表格对话中没有出现，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before Friday?

options.A: Submit her transcript request immediately
options.B: Get a new driver's license issued
options.C: Return on Thursday to confirm the correction
options.D: File a complaint about the error

questions.explanation: 男士建议女士先别提交成绩单申请，周四再回来确认更正已完成，女士接受了这个安排，所以C正确。A立即提交申请正是男士建议她暂缓的事，属于rejected_option。B重新办驾照毫无依据，她的驾照拼写本来就是对的，属于over_inference。D投诉错误对话里没有任何线索，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff why a scholarship disbursement hasn't appeared in her account yet

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my merit scholarship was supposed to post to my account two weeks ago, but it's still not showing up. Tuition is due Friday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me pull up your file. Hmm, okay. It looks like your scholarship is on hold because we're missing one document.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Missing? I'm pretty sure I turned everything in back in August.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You submitted almost everything. But we never received your signed enrollment verification form. Without it, the funds can't release.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. I had no idea. Where do I even get that form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the financial-aid portal under 'Required Documents.' Fill it out, sign it, and bring it back today. Then I can release the funds before the tuition deadline.

questions.stem: Why is the woman's scholarship on hold?

options.A: Her tuition payment was late
options.B: A required form was never submitted
options.C: Her grades dropped below the minimum
options.D: The scholarship budget ran out

questions.explanation: 男士查到奖学金被冻结是因为缺少一份文件——女士没有提交签字的入学验证表（enrollment verification form），所以B正确。A学费晚交是对话中提到的截止日期问题，但不是冻结奖学金的原因，属于misattributed。C成绩下降从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D奖学金预算用完对话里没有任何依据，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Pay her tuition with a personal loan
options.B: Wait for the scholarship to post automatically
options.C: Appeal the scholarship decision
options.D: Download and submit the verification form today

questions.explanation: 男士告诉女士去财务援助门户网站的'Required Documents'下载表格，填好签字今天带回来，女士也接受了，所以D正确。A用个人贷款交学费对话里完全没提，属于unrelated_next_step。B等奖学金自动到账与男士说的'必须先交表才能放款'直接矛盾，属于rejected_option。C申诉奖学金决定毫无依据，问题只是缺一份表而非决定被撤销，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff about a leaking radiator in her dorm room and when it can be repaired

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, the radiator in my room, 214 in Foster Hall, has been leaking water onto the floor all night. There's a puddle now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yikes, that's not good. I'll file an urgent maintenance ticket right away. Did you put a towel down to stop it from spreading?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, I've got a few towels under it. But I'm worried about the wood floor warping.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good thinking. A technician should be able to come by this afternoon. In the meantime, please keep the radiator valve turned off completely.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's already off. Should I move my stuff away from that wall too?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. Shift anything valuable to the other side of the room, and we'll have someone there within a few hours.

questions.stem: What has the woman already done about the leak?

options.A: Moved all her furniture to the hallway
options.B: Called an outside plumbing company
options.C: Placed towels under the radiator
options.D: Turned the heat up to dry the floor

questions.explanation: 女士说她已经在暖气片下面垫了几条毛巾（'I've got a few towels under it'），所以C正确。A把家具搬到走廊是错的，男士只是建议她把贵重物品挪到房间另一侧，并非走廊，属于wrong_detail。B叫外面的管道公司从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D把暖气开大烘干地板与她已经关掉阀门相矛盾，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do right after this conversation?

options.A: Move her valuable items away from the wall
options.B: Wait outside until the technician arrives
options.C: Mop up the puddle and reopen the valve
options.D: Request a transfer to a different room

questions.explanation: 对话结尾男士建议女士把贵重物品挪到房间另一侧，女士先前也主动问要不要挪东西，所以她接下来最可能挪开靠墙的物品，A正确。B在外面等技术员毫无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。C重新打开阀门与男士明确要求'把阀门完全关掉'直接矛盾，属于rejected_option。D申请换房在对话中从未涉及，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks library staff to recall a research book that another patron has had out past the due date

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, there's a book I need for my thesis, but the catalog says it's checked out and overdue. Can I get it recalled?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, I can place a recall. That gives the current borrower seven days to return it. What's the title?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's 'Coastal Sediment Dynamics.' But seven days? My draft is due in five. That won't work.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. In that case, let me check if another library in our consortium has a copy we can request through interlibrary loan.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that'd be great. How fast is interlibrary loan, usually?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: For a partner campus, usually two to three days. I'll put in the request now, and we'll email you the moment it arrives at the pickup desk.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main problem?

options.A: She owes a fine on an overdue book
options.B: She cannot find the book in the catalog
options.C: She wants to extend her own borrowing period
options.D: She needs a book that someone else has checked out

questions.explanation: 女士的核心问题是她论文要用的书被别人借走且已逾期，她拿不到，所以D正确。A欠逾期罚款说的是别人借的书逾期，不是她欠罚款，属于misattributed。B在目录里找不到书是错的，目录显示了书只是状态为借出，属于wrong_detail。C延长自己的借阅期与情况相反，书根本不在她手上，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What does the man decide is the better solution?

options.A: Placing a recall on the overdue book
options.B: Telling her to buy the book online
options.C: Requesting the book through interlibrary loan
options.D: Asking the current borrower to return it early

questions.explanation: 因为召回需要七天而女士只剩五天，男士转而决定通过馆际互借（interlibrary loan）从联盟内另一所图书馆调书，两三天就能到，所以C正确。A召回逾期书是男士最初提的方案，但因为时间不够被放弃了，属于rejected_option。B让她网上买书对话中从未出现，属于unrelated_next_step。D请当前借书人提前还书男士没有提议这么做，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks IT staff why she can't log into the campus exam-proctoring software before a timed online quiz

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I have a timed quiz in an hour, but the proctoring software keeps rejecting my login. It says 'account not recognized.'

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, don't panic. That usually means your account hasn't synced with the proctoring system yet. Are you using your full student email to log in?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I think so. Wait, I've been typing just my username without the '@university' part.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's almost certainly it. The proctoring tool needs the complete email address, not just the username.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a relief. Let me try logging in with the full address now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Go ahead. If it still won't let you in, call me back and I'll manually reset your access before the quiz starts.

questions.stem: What does the man identify as the likely cause of the problem?

options.A: The quiz had already started without her
options.B: She was not entering her complete email address
options.C: Her password had expired overnight
options.D: The proctoring server was down for maintenance

questions.explanation: 男士判断问题几乎肯定是女士登录时只输了用户名，没加完整的邮箱地址（缺'@university'部分），监考工具需要完整邮箱，所以B正确。A测验已经开始与'还有一小时考试'的设定矛盾，属于wrong_detail。C密码过期男士从未提到，错误信息是'账号无法识别'而非密码问题，属于related_but_not_stated。D服务器维护停机对话里没有任何依据，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will happen if the woman still cannot log in?

options.A: She will have to reschedule the quiz
options.B: The man will email her a new password
options.C: The man will manually reset her access
options.D: She will take the quiz in the IT office

questions.explanation: 男士明确说如果还是登不进去，让女士回电，他会在测验开始前手动重置她的访问权限，所以C正确。A重新安排测验对话中没提，属于unrelated_next_step。B发新密码是错的，男士说的是手动重置访问权限而非发密码，且问题本来也不是密码，属于wrong_detail。D去IT办公室考试男士从未这样建议，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks dining-services staff whether the dining hall can accommodate a nut allergy at the salad bar

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I have a severe nut allergy, and I'm a little nervous about the salad bar. Are the toppings safe for me?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good question. Most are fine, but the salad bar does have walnuts and almonds in open bins, so cross-contact is a real risk.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that worries me. The serving spoons get mixed up all the time.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. So instead, I'd recommend our allergen-free station near the back. Everything there is prepared separately, away from nuts.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I didn't know we had one of those. That sounds much safer.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It is. Just tell the server your allergy, and they'll make you a fresh plate with clean utensils. No cross-contact at all.

questions.stem: Why does the man warn the woman about the salad bar?

options.A: It has open bins of nuts nearby
options.B: It closes earlier than the other stations
options.C: It charges extra for the toppings
options.D: It only serves cooked vegetables

questions.explanation: 男士警告说沙拉吧有装在敞口容器里的核桃和杏仁，存在交叉接触的真实风险，所以A正确。B沙拉吧关得早从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。C配料额外收费对话中没有任何线索，属于related_but_not_stated。D只供应熟蔬菜与沙拉吧的设定不符，也从未提到，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do for her meal?

options.A: Skip the dining hall and eat in her room
options.B: Carefully pick safe toppings from the salad bar
options.C: Ask the chef to wash the salad bar spoons
options.D: Go to the allergen-free station and tell the server

questions.explanation: 男士推荐后面的无过敏原专区，告诉服务员过敏情况就能拿到用干净餐具现做的一盘，女士觉得安全多了，所以D正确。A回房间吃对话中没提，属于unrelated_next_step。B在沙拉吧小心挑配料与男士'别用沙拉吧、去专区'的建议相反，属于rejected_option。C让厨师洗沙拉吧的勺子不是给出的方案，男士的方案是换去专区，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks an academic advisor whether to drop a difficult course or switch it to pass/fail before the deadline

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm really struggling in organic chemistry. I'm thinking about dropping it, but I'm not sure that's the right move.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let's think it through. Is organic chem required for your major, or is it an elective?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's required for my biology track. So if I drop it, I'd have to retake it next year anyway.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. In that case, dropping just delays the problem. Have you considered switching it to pass/fail instead? You'd still earn the credit.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I didn't realize that was an option this late. Would it hurt my GPA?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No, that's the beauty of it. A pass won't affect your GPA at all, and you'd still satisfy the requirement. The deadline's tomorrow, though, so decide soon.

questions.stem: What is the woman trying to decide?

options.A: Whether to change her major to biology
options.B: How to handle a course she's struggling in
options.C: Which elective to take next semester
options.D: Whether to hire a chemistry tutor

questions.explanation: 对话核心是女士在有机化学这门课上很吃力，正在纠结怎么处理这门课——退掉还是改成通过/不通过制，所以B正确。A换专业为生物学是错的，她已经在生物方向且只是讨论一门课，属于reversed_situation。C选下学期哪门选修课没被讨论，且这门课是必修不是选修，属于wrong_topic。D请化学家教对话中从未提及，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Switch the course to pass/fail before tomorrow
options.B: Drop the course and retake it next year
options.C: Change to a different academic track
options.D: Ask the professor for extra credit

questions.explanation: 顾问指出退课只是推迟问题，而改成通过/不通过制既能拿到学分又不影响GPA，还满足必修要求，且截止日期是明天，女士得到了满意的答案，所以她最可能选这个方案，A正确。B退课明年重修正是顾问劝阻的'只是推迟问题'，属于rejected_option。C换学术方向对话里没提，属于unrelated_next_step。D找教授要附加分毫无依据，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks a bookstore clerk whether a lab manual comes bundled with an access code or must be bought separately

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm buying this physics lab manual. Does it come with the online access code, or do I have to buy that separately?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good thing you asked. The used copies don't include a code, only the brand-new shrink-wrapped ones do.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I grabbed a used one to save money. So this won't have a code inside?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right, that used one won't. You can either swap it for a new bundle, or buy a standalone access code online for about the same total.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. If the price is basically the same, I'd rather just get the new bundle and keep it simple.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Makes sense. Let me grab a sealed copy from the back for you, and you'll be all set with the code included.

questions.stem: What does the clerk tell the woman about the used copies?

options.A: They are out of stock today
options.B: They cost more than the new ones
options.C: They do not include an access code
options.D: They are missing several pages

questions.explanation: 店员说二手书不含访问码，只有全新的塑封本才有，所以C正确。A今天缺货是错的，女士已经拿了一本二手的在手上，属于wrong_detail。B二手比全新贵与常识及对话相悖，店员说两种方案总价'差不多'，属于wrong_detail。D缺页对话中从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Buy the new shrink-wrapped bundle
options.B: Keep the used manual and skip the code
options.C: Order the access code from another website
options.D: Borrow the manual from a classmate

questions.explanation: 女士说既然价格基本一样，她宁愿直接买全新套装图省事，店员也去后面拿密封本了，所以A正确。B留着二手书不要密码与她明确选择换全新套装相反，属于rejected_option。C从别的网站单独买密码是被提到但被女士放弃的选项，属于rejected_option。D向同学借手册对话中从未出现，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: a student asks rec-center staff how to reserve a squash court and whether equipment can be rented on site

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to book a squash court for this weekend. Do I reserve it here at the desk, or is there an app?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's all through the rec-center app now. You log in, pick 'Squash Courts,' and choose an open slot. Booking opens 48 hours ahead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. And I don't have a racket. Can I rent one here?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yep, we rent rackets and goggles right at this desk. Just show your student ID when you pick them up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. Is there a rental fee?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Rackets are free for students; we only ask for a two-dollar deposit on the goggles, which you get back when you return them.

questions.stem: How does the man say the woman should reserve a court?

options.A: Through the rec-center app
options.B: By signing a sheet at the desk
options.C: By calling the front office
options.D: Through her course portal

questions.explanation: 男士说预订壁球场现在全部通过康乐中心的app，登录后选'Squash Courts'再挑空档，所以A正确。B在前台签纸是错的，男士明确说现在改成用app了，属于wrong_detail。C打电话给前台办公室从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D通过课程门户预订与场地预订无关，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely need to do to rent a racket?

options.A: Pay a two-dollar deposit for it
options.B: Reserve it 48 hours in advance
options.C: Show her student ID at the desk
options.D: Sign a liability waiver first

questions.explanation: 男士说在前台租球拍和护目镜，取的时候出示学生证就行，所以租球拍最可能需要出示学生证，C正确。A交两美元押金是针对护目镜的，球拍对学生免费，属于wrong_detail/misattributed。B提前48小时预订说的是订场地，不是租球拍，属于misattributed。D先签免责声明对话中从未提及，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks health-center staff how to get a flu shot and whether an appointment is required

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to get a flu shot. Do I need to make an appointment, or can I just walk in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: For flu shots specifically, we run walk-in clinics every weekday from one to four in the afternoon. No appointment needed.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's convenient. Is there anything I should bring?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just your student ID and your insurance card. The shot itself is free for enrolled students, so there's no charge.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. I have a class until two-thirty today. Could I still come after that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Absolutely. As long as you're here before four, you'll be fine. I'd come a little early, though, since it gets busy around three.

questions.stem: What does the man say about getting a flu shot?

options.A: It requires booking three days ahead
options.B: It is only offered on weekends
options.C: No appointment is needed during walk-in hours
options.D: It costs a small fee for students

questions.explanation: 男士说打流感疫苗有每个工作日下午一点到四点的免预约门诊，不需要预约，所以C正确。A提前三天预约与'免预约'直接矛盾，属于wrong_detail。B只在周末提供是错的，门诊是每个工作日，属于wrong_detail。D对学生收少量费用与男士说的'对在校生免费'相悖，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do today?

options.A: Come in for her shot after her class ends
options.B: Schedule an appointment for next week
options.C: Pay for the shot at the front desk
options.D: Get the shot first thing in the morning

questions.explanation: 女士说她今天有课上到两点半，男士确认只要四点前到都行，但建议早点来因为三点左右人多，所以女士最可能下课后过来打针，A正确。B约下周与她今天就想打、且是免预约门诊矛盾，属于rejected_option。C在前台付费与'对学生免费'相悖，属于wrong_detail。D一大早来打针与她两点半才下课的时间冲突，属于wrong_detail。

items.situation: two students compare notes on how to fix an enrollment hold blocking their registration

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ugh, I just tried to register for spring classes and the system blocked me. It says I have some kind of hold on my account.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that happened to me last week. Was it a financial hold or an advising hold?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It says 'advising hold.' I have no idea what that even means.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It just means you have to meet with your advisor and get them to sign off before the registrar lets you enroll. It's annoying but quick.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? So I can't just clear it online myself?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nope. You have to book a slot on the advising portal. They release the hold right after the meeting, usually within an hour.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'll book one for this afternoon then. Thanks, that saves me a trip to the registrar.

questions.stem: What kind of hold is on the woman's account?

options.A: An advising hold
options.B: A financial hold
options.C: A library fine hold
options.D: A housing hold

questions.explanation: 女生明确说账户上写着 'advising hold'（选修指导锁定），所以 A 正确。B 财务锁定是男生一开始问的两种可能之一，但女生否定了，属于改变关键事实的干扰项。C 图书馆罚款锁定和 D 住宿锁定在对话中从未提及，属于相关但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Go to the registrar's office in person
options.B: Pay an outstanding balance online
options.C: Schedule a meeting with her advisor
options.D: Wait for the hold to clear automatically

questions.explanation: 女生最后说她要在指导预约系统上约今天下午的一个时段，也就是去见她的指导老师，所以 C 正确。A 去注册办公室是她明确说省掉的一趟，属于被拒绝的选项。B 在线付清欠款对应的是财务锁定，而她是 advising hold，属于不相关的下一步。D 等待自动解除与男生说必须先开会才能解除相矛盾，属于过度推断。

items.situation: a student asks library staff whether a course-reserve book can be checked out overnight before an exam

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I need the statistics textbook that's on course reserve. Can I take it home tonight? My exam is tomorrow morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Reserve books normally stay in the building, but the overnight loan starts at six p.m. You can take it then and return it by ten the next morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, perfect. It's almost five-thirty now, so I'll just wait around.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sounds good. Just a heads-up though, the late fee on reserve items is steep, two dollars an hour after ten.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yikes, two dollars an hour? I'll set an alarm to make sure I bring it back on time.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. And bring your student ID when you check it out, the system won't process the loan without it.

questions.stem: When can the man take the reserve book out of the library?

options.A: Starting at six in the evening
options.B: Immediately, at five-thirty
options.C: Only the next morning
options.D: After he pays a deposit

questions.explanation: 馆员说过夜借阅从晚上六点开始，所以 A 正确。B 立刻借（五点半）是错的——男生说现在快五点半了要等，正说明还不能借，属于改变关键时间的干扰项。C 只能第二天早上借与六点开始借相矛盾，属于改变关键事实。D 交押金从未被提及，属于相关但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: Why does the man say he will set an alarm?

options.A: To wake up early for his exam
options.B: To remember to start studying
options.C: To avoid an expensive late fee
options.D: To return the book by six p.m.

questions.explanation: 馆员提醒过夜逾期罚款很贵，每小时两美元，男生听到后说要设闹钟确保按时还书，所以闹钟是为了避免昂贵罚款，C 正确。A 为考试早起、B 提醒开始学习在对话中都没有依据，属于不相关的下一步。D 在六点前还书是错的——还书截止是第二天上午十点，不是六点，属于改变关键事实。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff about signing up for summer dorm storage instead of hauling everything home

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm flying home for the summer and I really don't want to drag all my stuff to the airport. Does housing offer any storage?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We do! There's a summer storage program. You get one labeled bin, and we keep it in a locked room in the basement until you're back in the fall.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's exactly what I need. Is it free?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's twenty dollars for the whole summer. You sign up at the front desk and we give you the bin and a sticker with your room number on it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Twenty dollars is totally worth it. Can I sign up right now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, but bring whatever you want stored within the next two weeks, that's when the storage room closes for the summer.

questions.stem: Why does the woman talk to the man?

options.A: To ship her belongings to the airport
options.B: To find a place to store her things over summer
options.C: To request a different dorm room for the fall
options.D: To complain about a locked basement room

questions.explanation: 女生说她暑假要飞回家，不想把所有东西拖去机场，问宿舍有没有存储服务，所以核心是给东西找暑期存放处，B 正确。A 把东西寄到机场是对她原话的曲解——她正是不想拖去机场，属于颠倒情境。C 申请秋季换房间从未提及，属于另一个话题。D 抱怨地下室上锁也没有发生，地下室只是存储地点，属于错误话题。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do soon?

options.A: Bring her items in within two weeks
options.B: Pay the fee at the airport
options.C: Move into a basement room
options.D: Wait until fall to sign up

questions.explanation: 男生最后提醒她要在接下来两周内把要存的东西拿来，因为存储室那时就关闭过暑假了，所以 A 正确。B 在机场付费没有依据，费用是在前台交，属于不相关的下一步。C 搬进地下室房间是误解——地下室是存东西的地方，不是住的地方，属于过度推断。D 等到秋天再报名与马上报名、两周内送东西相矛盾，属于被拒绝的选项。

items.situation: a student asks IT staff how to connect a laptop to the campus printing system after the print queue keeps failing

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to print my lab report from my laptop, but every time I send it, the job just disappears from the queue. Nothing comes out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you installed the campus print driver? A lot of people skip that and the jobs silently fail.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I don't think I have. I just connected to the printer through the Wi-Fi settings.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's the issue. You need to download the driver from the IT portal first, then add the printer using the campus queue, not directly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ah, okay. Is the driver hard to install?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Not at all. There's a step-by-step guide on the portal. Takes about five minutes, and then your print jobs will route correctly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great, I'll go do that now. Thanks for figuring out what I was missing.

questions.stem: Why does the woman say the man's print jobs are failing?

options.A: He has not installed the campus print driver
options.B: The printer is out of paper
options.C: His laptop is not connected to Wi-Fi
options.D: The IT portal is temporarily down

questions.explanation: 女生指出问题在于男生没有安装校园打印驱动，只是通过 Wi-Fi 直接连了打印机，所以 A 正确。B 打印机缺纸从未提及，属于相关但未陈述。C 笔记本没连 Wi-Fi 是错的——男生明确说他通过 Wi-Fi 设置连了打印机，属于改变关键事实。D 门户网站宕机也没提到，门户其实是用来下载驱动的地方，属于相关但未陈述。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Download the driver from the IT portal
options.B: Buy a personal printer for his dorm
options.C: Ask a classmate to print the report
options.D: Reconnect to the printer through Wi-Fi

questions.explanation: 男生最后说他现在就去做女生建议的事，也就是从 IT 门户下载驱动并按指南安装，所以 A 正确。B 自己买打印机、C 让同学帮忙打印在对话里都没有依据，属于不相关的下一步。D 再通过 Wi-Fi 连打印机恰恰是女生指出的错误做法，男生不会重复，属于被拒绝的选项。

items.situation: a student asks dining-hall staff how to use leftover meal-plan swipes before they expire at the end of the term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just realized I have like thirty meal swipes left and the semester ends Friday. Do they roll over to next term?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Unfortunately no, swipes reset to zero at the end of each semester. They don't carry over.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, that's a lot of wasted money. Is there anything I can do with them?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, yes. This week you can use swipes at the campus market to grab packaged snacks and drinks, not just hot meals.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? I can basically stock up on groceries?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Pretty much. Each swipe covers up to ten dollars of market items. It's the best way to not lose them.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's awesome. I'm heading to the market right after this.

questions.stem: What happens to the woman's unused meal swipes at the end of the semester?

options.A: They are refunded to her account
options.B: They roll over to the next term
options.C: They can be transferred to a friend
options.D: They reset to zero and are lost

questions.explanation: 男生明确说餐卡刷次在每学期末归零，不会结转，所以 D 正确。A 退款到账户从未提及，属于相关但未陈述。B 结转到下学期正是男生否定的说法，属于改变关键事实。C 转给朋友也没有提到，属于相关但未陈述。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Ask for a refund on the unused swipes
options.B: Go to the campus market to use her swipes
options.C: Save the swipes for next semester
options.D: Eat several hot meals before Friday

questions.explanation: 女生最后说她聊完就直接去校园市场，去用刷次买打包零食和饮料，所以 B 正确。A 要求退款没有依据，男生从没说能退款，属于不相关的下一步。C 把刷次留到下学期与归零、不结转相矛盾，属于被拒绝的选项。D 在周五前吃几顿热饭虽可行，但女生明显被市场购物吸引而非堆着吃热餐，属于过度推断。

items.situation: a student asks an academic advisor whether to take an extra elective or keep a lighter schedule before graduation

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm trying to decide my last-semester schedule. I've technically met all my graduation requirements, but I'm tempted to add an extra elective in data visualization.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's a nice problem to have. What's pulling you toward the extra course?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it'd look good for the kind of analyst jobs I'm applying to. But I'm also working twenty hours a week, so I'm worried about burning out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's a real concern. Have you considered auditing it instead? You'd attend and learn the material without the pressure of graded assignments.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Huh, I hadn't thought of that. So I'd still get the exposure without it dragging down my GPA if I'm stretched thin?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. And if you find you love it and have the bandwidth, you can switch to taking it for credit in the first two weeks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That actually sounds like the perfect middle ground. Let me look into how auditing works.

questions.stem: What is the man's main concern in the conversation?

options.A: He has not met his graduation requirements
options.B: Whether to add an extra course in his final semester
options.C: How to find an analyst job after graduation
options.D: Whether to reduce his part-time work hours

questions.explanation: 男生开头说在纠结最后一学期要不要加一门数据可视化选修，整段都围绕这个决定展开，所以 B 正确。A 没满足毕业要求是错的——他说自己技术上已满足全部毕业要求，属于颠倒情境。C 找分析师工作只是他想加课的理由之一，不是对话核心，属于以偏概全。D 减少兼职工时从未被讨论，他只是提到打工二十小时担心累垮，属于错误话题。

questions.stem: What does the advisor suggest as a compromise?

options.A: Dropping one of his required courses
options.B: Quitting his part-time job for the semester
options.C: Applying to analyst jobs after he graduates
options.D: Auditing the course instead of taking it for credit

questions.explanation: 顾问建议他旁听这门课——去上课、学内容，但不承受打分作业的压力，男生称这是完美的折中方案，所以 D 正确。A 退掉一门必修课与他已满足毕业要求、只是考虑加课相矛盾，属于过度推断。B 整学期辞掉兼职从未被建议，属于不相关的下一步。C 毕业后申请分析师工作是男生加课的动机，不是顾问提的折中办法，属于不相关的下一步。

items.situation: a student asks bookstore staff whether the assigned edition of a textbook can be swapped for a cheaper older edition

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, the chemistry textbook on the shelf is the fifth edition and it's ninety dollars. Is the older fourth edition okay to use instead? It's way cheaper online.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That depends on the professor. Some courses use the homework problems built into the new edition's online code, and the old one won't have access.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't think about the online code. How would I find out if my class needs it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Check your syllabus, it usually says whether the access code is required. Or email the professor directly to be safe.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good idea. If the code isn't required, the fourth edition would save me a ton.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. The content is nearly identical, just the page numbers differ. But don't buy anything until you've confirmed.

questions.stem: Why might the older edition not work for the woman's course?

options.A: It does not include the online access code
options.B: It is missing several entire chapters
options.C: It is more expensive than the new edition
options.D: The bookstore does not stock it

questions.explanation: 男生解释有些课用新版自带的在线作业代码，旧版没有这个访问权限，所以 A 正确。B 缺好几整章是错的——男生说内容几乎完全相同，只是页码不同，属于改变关键事实。C 旧版更贵与女生说旧版网上便宜得多相矛盾，属于颠倒情境。D 书店不卖旧版从未提及，属于相关但未陈述。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before buying a textbook?

options.A: Buy the fourth edition immediately online
options.B: Check whether the access code is required
options.C: Switch to a different chemistry course
options.D: Buy the fifth edition from the shelf

questions.explanation: 男生建议她查教学大纲或直接给教授发邮件，确认是否需要访问代码，并叮嘱确认前先别买，女生也认可，所以她会先确认代码是否必需，B 正确。A 立刻在网上买旧版与男生说确认前别买相矛盾，属于被拒绝的选项。C 换一门化学课从未提及，属于不相关的下一步。D 直接买货架上的第五版无视了她想省钱、要先确认的整个讨论，属于过度推断。

items.situation: a student asks gym staff how to reserve a basketball court during busy evening hours

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hey, my friends and I want to play basketball tonight, but the courts are always packed after dinner. Can we reserve one ahead of time?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You can! Evening courts are reservation-only after six. You book a one-hour slot through the rec center app.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice. Is there a limit on how often we can book?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: One slot per person per day. So if a few of you each book, you could chain together a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. We'll do that. Do we need to bring anything to check in?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Just your student ID. Scan it at the court entrance and your reservation pops up automatically.

questions.stem: How does the man reserve a basketball court for the evening?

options.A: Through the rec center app
options.B: By signing a clipboard at the court
options.C: By calling the gym front desk
options.D: By emailing the rec center staff

questions.explanation: 女生说六点后晚间球场只能预约，要通过康乐中心的 app 订一小时时段，所以 A 正确。B 在球场签写字板、C 打电话给前台、D 给工作人员发邮件在对话中都没有提及，都是相关但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: Why does the woman mention that each person can book one slot per day?

options.A: To warn the man that courts are fully booked
options.B: To suggest the man play at a different time
options.C: So the friends can combine slots for more court time
options.D: Because the man has already used his booking

questions.explanation: 女生说每人每天一个时段，接着说如果几个人各订一个就能连起来玩几个小时，意思是让朋友们拼时段获得更长的打球时间，所以 C 正确。A 警告球场全订满与她在教他怎么订相矛盾，属于过度推断。B 建议换时间打没有依据，属于不相关的下一步。D 男生已用完预约是错的——对话里他还没订过，属于改变关键事实。

items.situation: a student asks health-center staff how to pick up a prescription refill before the pharmacy window closes

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I need to refill my allergy prescription. Can I just pick it up at the campus pharmacy window today?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You can, but refills aren't automatic. You have to request it first, and then the pharmacy needs a few hours to fill it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: A few hours? The window closes at five, and it's already two.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then let's submit the refill request right now through the health portal. If we do it immediately, it should be ready just before closing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, please put it in now. I'd hate to come back tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Done. I'll mark it urgent. You'll get a text when it's ready, so just watch your phone.

questions.stem: Why can't the woman pick up her refill right away?

options.A: The pharmacy needs a few hours to fill it
options.B: The pharmacy window is already closed
options.C: She does not have a valid prescription
options.D: The medication is out of stock

questions.explanation: 男生说续药不是自动的，要先申请，药房还需要几个小时来配药，所以不能立刻取，A 正确。B 药房窗口已经关了是错的——窗口五点关，现在才两点，属于改变关键事实。C 她没有有效处方在对话里没有依据，她要的是续配已有的过敏药，属于相关但未陈述。D 药品缺货也从未提及，属于相关但未陈述。

questions.stem: How will the woman know her prescription is ready?

options.A: She will receive a text message
options.B: She will get an email notification
options.C: The pharmacy will call her dorm
options.D: She has to keep checking in person

questions.explanation: 男生说会把它标记为加急，准备好后她会收到一条短信，让她看手机，所以 A 正确。B 收到邮件通知是错的——他说的是短信，不是邮件，属于改变关键事实。C 药房打电话到宿舍同样把通知方式弄错了，属于改变关键事实。D 必须一直亲自去问与他说会发短信通知相矛盾，属于被拒绝的选项。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff how to apply for a one-time emergency grant after an unexpected expense

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, my laptop died right before finals and I can't afford a replacement. Someone mentioned the school has emergency funding?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We do, there's an emergency grant for exactly this kind of situation. It's a one-time award, and you don't pay it back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that would be a lifesaver. What do I have to do to apply?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You fill out a short form describing the expense, and you attach some proof, like a repair quote or a receipt.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I don't have a receipt yet since I haven't bought anything. Would a price estimate from the computer store work?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Absolutely, an estimate is fine. Once you submit, the committee usually reviews requests within three business days.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three days, okay. I'll grab an estimate from the store this afternoon and submit tonight.

questions.stem: Why is the man speaking with the financial-aid staff?

options.A: To repay a loan he received earlier
options.B: To apply for emergency funding after his laptop broke
options.C: To report that his scholarship payment is late
options.D: To ask for an extension on his finals

questions.explanation: 男生说期末前笔记本坏了又买不起新的，听说学校有应急资金，整段都在问怎么申请应急补助，所以 B 正确。A 偿还之前的贷款是错的——应急补助是一次性的、不用还，属于颠倒情境。C 报告奖学金到款晚了从未提及，属于错误话题。D 申请期末考试延期也没有发生，他要的是钱不是延期，属于错误话题。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do this afternoon?

options.A: Buy a new laptop from the store
options.B: Wait for the committee's decision
options.C: Submit a receipt for a completed purchase
options.D: Get a price estimate from the computer store

questions.explanation: 男生最后说今天下午去店里拿一份报价，今晚提交申请，所以 D 正确。A 直接买新笔记本是错的——他还没钱买，正等补助，而且他打算先拿报价而非购买，属于被拒绝的选项。B 等委员会决定与他下午就去拿报价、今晚提交的计划相矛盾，时间顺序在前，属于过度推断。C 提交已完成购买的收据是错的——他明说还没买东西、没有收据，只能用报价，属于改变关键事实。

items.situation: a student asks the registrar to fix a wrong birth date on her enrollment record before a study-abroad application

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I noticed my birth date is wrong on my enrollment record. It says March instead of May, and I need it fixed before my study-abroad application closes Friday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, that's a personal-data correction. We can't change those just from a verbal request, though. You'll need to bring in some official proof.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. Like what exactly?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A passport works best, or a birth certificate. Once you drop that off here, the update goes through within two business days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Two days? But my application deadline is Friday, and today's already Wednesday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then bring the passport in first thing tomorrow morning. If we log it before noon, I can flag it as urgent and have it corrected by Friday.

questions.stem: What does the man say the woman must provide?

options.A: A signed verbal statement at the desk
options.B: An official document such as a passport
options.C: An email from her study-abroad office
options.D: A new enrollment form filled out online

questions.explanation: 男士明确说改个人信息需要官方证明，建议带护照或出生证明，所以B正确。A错误：他特意说不能仅凭口头请求(verbal request)就更改。C错误：对话从未提到学习办公室的邮件。D错误：从未提及在线填写新的注册表，只说带证件来。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Cancel her study-abroad application
options.B: Mail her birth certificate to the office
options.C: Bring her passport to the office tomorrow morning
options.D: Wait until after Friday to fix the record

questions.explanation: 男士建议明早第一时间带护照来，并说中午前登记就能加急在周五前改好，这是给她的唯一可行方案，故C正确。A错误：她想赶在截止日期前完成申请，不会取消，属过度推断。B错误：对话说的是当面drop off证件，没提邮寄。D错误：她的截止日期就是周五，等周五之后毫无意义，与情境相反。

items.situation: a student asks library staff how to access a journal article that is behind a paywall through the library subscription

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to read an article for my psychology paper, but the publisher's site wants me to pay thirty dollars. Does the library have access?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Probably. Don't pay for it. What's the journal name?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's the Journal of Cognitive Development.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, we subscribe to that one. The trick is you have to reach it through the library's database portal, not the public website. Otherwise it won't recognize you as a student.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that makes sense. So I just search for it inside the portal?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. Log into the portal, search the journal title, and the full text downloads free. If it still asks for payment, you're on the wrong site.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the library staff?

options.A: She cannot access an article without paying a fee
options.B: She wants to subscribe to a psychology journal
options.C: She lost the login for her psychology course site
options.D: She needs to renew an overdue journal issue

questions.explanation: 女士说她想读一篇文章但出版商网站要收三十美元，问图书馆是否有权限，核心问题就是无法免费访问，故A正确。B错误：她不是想自己订阅期刊，是图书馆已订阅，属误读情境。C错误：对话从未提到丢失课程网站登录。D错误：完全没提到逾期或续借期刊，属无关话题。

questions.stem: What does the man imply if the site still asks for payment?

options.A: The library's subscription has expired
options.B: The student must pay the thirty-dollar fee
options.C: The journal is not available anywhere
options.D: The student is using the wrong website

questions.explanation: 男士最后说如果还要收费'你就是在错误的网站上'，暗示要通过门户而非公开网站访问，故D正确。A错误：他明确说图书馆订阅了该期刊，没说过期，属过度推断。B错误：他一开始就让她'别付钱'，与建议相反，属被否决的选项。C错误：他说图书馆有订阅，期刊是可获取的，与事实相反。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff how to switch from a triple room to a double after a roommate moved out

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, one of my two roommates moved out last week, so now there are only two of us in a triple. Can we switch to an actual double room?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Possibly, but it depends on what's open. Honestly, a lot of students would rather stay in a triple that's only got two people in it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Why's that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Because you keep all the extra space but only pay the triple rate, which is cheaper per person. If you move to a real double, your rent actually goes up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh, I hadn't thought about the cost. I just assumed a smaller room would be cheaper.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's the opposite, actually. I'd talk it over with your roommate before you decide. There's no rush — the room's yours either way.

questions.stem: According to the man, what is the advantage of staying in the triple?

options.A: It is closer to the campus center
options.B: The room will be renovated soon
options.C: They keep extra space at a lower rate
options.D: They can choose a third roommate freely

questions.explanation: 男士说留在三人间能保留多余空间但只付较便宜的三人间费用，故C正确。A错误：对话从未提到房间离校园中心的远近，属无关细节。B错误：完全没提到装修，属虚构信息。D错误：他没说可以自由挑选第三个室友，只说现在两人住三人间，属未提及。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do before deciding?

options.A: Discuss the choice with her roommate
options.B: Pay the higher double-room rate now
options.C: Request a room near the campus center
options.D: Move out of the triple immediately

questions.explanation: 男士说'我建议你先和室友商量再决定，不用急'，故A正确。B错误：他在解释搬到双人间反而更贵，并未让她现在就付更高费用，属被否决的方向。C错误：位置问题对话从未涉及，属无关下一步。D错误：他明确说'不用急，房间反正是你们的'，立刻搬走与他的建议相反。

items.situation: a student asks IT staff why the campus Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting her laptop every few minutes

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my laptop keeps dropping off the campus Wi-Fi every few minutes. It's driving me crazy during lectures. Is the network down?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The network's fine. Are you connected to the guest Wi-Fi by any chance, instead of the secure student one?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Um, I'm not sure. I just clicked whatever popped up when I first got here.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the issue. The guest network logs you out automatically every fifteen minutes. You want to connect to 'CampusSecure' using your student ID and password.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh! So that's why. How do I switch over?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just forget the guest network in your settings, then select CampusSecure and log in. Once you do, it'll stay connected all day.

questions.stem: According to the man, why does the woman's laptop keep disconnecting?

options.A: Her laptop's wireless card is faulty
options.B: She is on the guest network, not the secure one
options.C: The campus network is temporarily down
options.D: Her student password has expired

questions.explanation: 男士判断她连的是访客网络而非安全的学生网络，访客网络每十五分钟自动登出，故B正确。A错误：他没说她的无线网卡有问题，属虚构原因。C错误：他一开始就说'网络没问题'，与事实相反。D错误：对话从未提到密码过期，属未提及的细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Take her laptop in for hardware repair
options.B: Reset her student account password
options.C: Connect to the CampusSecure network
options.D: Use a wired connection during lectures

questions.explanation: 男士告诉她忘记访客网络、选CampusSecure并登录就能整天保持连接，这是明确的解决步骤，故C正确。A错误：问题不在硬件，送修属过度推断。B错误：从未提到重置密码，属无关下一步。D错误：对话只讨论无线网络，没提有线连接，属未提及的方案。

items.situation: a student asks dining-services staff whether unused guest meal passes roll over to the next semester

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I've still got five guest meal passes left this semester. Do they roll over to next semester, or do I lose them?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Unfortunately, guest passes reset at the end of each term. They don't carry over.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a shame. So they'll just disappear?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Pretty much. But here's a tip — you've got two weeks left, so invite some friends to a meal before finals. Otherwise it's money down the drain.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good point. I'll definitely use them up then.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the guest meal passes?

options.A: They can be exchanged for cash
options.B: They can be shared between students
options.C: They do not carry over to next semester
options.D: They are valid for one more year

questions.explanation: 男士明确说访客餐券在每学期末重置，不会结转，故C正确。A错误：他说用不完就是'钱打水漂'，但从未说能换现金，属未提及。B错误：对话没说餐券可在学生间转让，只说可请朋友吃饭，属误读。D错误：他说餐券学期末就清零，并非再有效一年，属错误细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do with her passes?

options.A: Use them to treat friends before finals
options.B: Save them for the next semester
options.C: Return them to the dining office
options.D: Exchange them for a meal-plan refund

questions.explanation: 男士建议她趁还有两周请朋友吃饭，女士回应'好主意，我一定把它们用掉'，故A正确。B错误：餐券不能结转，她也接受了这点，存起来与对话相反，属被否决的选项。C错误：从未提到把餐券退回餐饮办公室，属无关下一步。D错误：对话没提退款，属未提及的方案。

items.situation: a student asks an academic advisor whether an online summer course will count toward her major requirements

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm thinking of taking an online statistics course over the summer. Will it count toward my economics major, or only as a general elective?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It can count toward the major, but only if it's the version with the lab component. The plain lecture-only one won't satisfy the requirement.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't realize there were two versions. How do I tell them apart?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Check the course code. The one ending in 'L' includes the lab. Make sure you register for that exact section, not the one without the L.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll double-check the code before I sign up.

questions.stem: According to the advisor, which version of the course counts toward the major?

options.A: The lecture-only version
options.B: The version with a lab component
options.C: Any version taken over the summer
options.D: The version offered only in person

questions.explanation: 顾问说只有带实验(lab)部分的版本才能算入专业要求，故B正确。A错误：他明确说纯讲授版本不满足要求，属误读。C错误：他强调不是任何版本都行，必须是带实验的，属过度概括。D错误：对话讨论的是在线课程，从未说必须线下面授，属虚构细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before registering?

options.A: Verify the course code ends in 'L'
options.B: Switch her major to statistics
options.C: Ask to take the lecture-only section
options.D: Cancel her summer course plans

questions.explanation: 顾问说结尾带'L'的课程含实验，要注册那一节；女士回应'我报名前会再核对课程代码'，故A正确。B错误：她只是想让暑期课算入经济学专业，没提换专业，属过度推断。C错误：纯讲授版不满足要求，选它与目标相反，属被否决的选项。D错误：她打算选课，不是取消计划，属相反情境。

items.situation: a student asks a bookstore clerk whether she can return a textbook she bought but no longer needs after dropping a course

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this chemistry textbook last week, but I just dropped the course. Can I return it for a refund?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, as long as it's within the return window. Do you still have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, it's right here. Oh, but I did write my name inside the front cover. Is that a problem?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, normally we can't take books that've been marked up. But just a name on the cover page should be fine — that's not really damage.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh good, what a relief. So I'll get the full amount back?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yep, full refund to your original payment card. It'll show up in a few business days.

questions.stem: What does the woman worry might prevent the return?

options.A: She has lost her original receipt
options.B: The return window has already closed
options.C: She wrote her name inside the cover
options.D: The textbook is the wrong edition

questions.explanation: 女士担心她在书的封面内页写了名字会不会有问题，故C正确。A错误：她说收据'就在这儿'，并没丢，属相反情境。B错误：店员问是否在退货期内，但女士没表达对此的担心，担心的是写名字，属误读。D错误：对话从未提到版本问题，属虚构细节。

questions.stem: What will most likely happen next?

options.A: The clerk will reject the marked-up book
options.B: The woman will receive a full refund
options.C: The woman will exchange it for another book
options.D: The clerk will charge a restocking fee

questions.explanation: 店员说封面写名字不算损坏没问题，并确认会全额退款到原付款卡，故B正确。A错误：他明确说封面写名字'没问题'，会接受这本书，与事实相反，属被否决的选项。C错误：对话谈的是退款不是换书，属无关下一步。D错误：他说是'全额退款'，没提任何手续费，属过度推断。

items.situation: a student asks rec-center staff why her gym membership card won't scan at the entrance turnstile

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my gym card won't scan at the turnstile. The light just keeps flashing red. I was here last week and it worked fine.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check your account. Hmm — it looks like your membership lapsed at the start of the month. Did you renew for this semester?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I thought it renewed automatically with my tuition.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It used to, but the policy changed. Now you have to opt in each semester through the rec-center website. That's why your card stopped working.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ugh, nobody told me. Can I renew right now at the desk?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You can, but it's actually cheaper online. I'd just renew on the website from your phone — takes two minutes and the card works instantly after.

questions.stem: Why won't the woman's gym card scan?

options.A: The turnstile scanner is broken
options.B: Her membership was not renewed this semester
options.C: She is using an old, expired card
