options.D: Her tuition payment is overdue

questions.explanation: 男士查账户后发现她的会员资格月初就失效了，因为现在每学期要主动续费，故B正确。A错误：问题在账户不在闸机扫描器，属误读。C错误：他没说卡本身过期或是旧卡，是会员资格没续，属错误细节。D错误：他说政策变了不再随学费自动续费，但没说学费逾期，属未提及。

questions.stem: What does the man recommend the woman do?

options.A: Renew her membership on the website
options.B: Pay the renewal fee at the front desk
options.C: Wait for the card to reactivate on its own
options.D: Report the broken turnstile to staff

questions.explanation: 男士说虽然能在前台续费，但网上更便宜，建议用手机在网站续费，两分钟就好且卡立即生效，故A正确。B错误：他说前台可以但网上更便宜，更推荐网上，前台是次选，属被否决的方向。C错误：卡不会自动重新激活，需要主动续费，属过度推断。D错误：闸机没坏，问题在会员资格，属无关下一步。

items.situation: a student asks health-center staff how to get a copy of her vaccination records for a study-abroad program

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my study-abroad program needs an official copy of my vaccination records. How do I get one from the health center?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. You can request them through the patient portal, under 'Medical Records.' We email an official PDF within about three days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Three days? My program needs them by Monday, and it's already Thursday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Cutting it close. In that case, fill out a records-release form here at the desk and mark it 'expedited.' Then we can have it ready by tomorrow afternoon.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's much better. Where do I get the form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll hand you one right now. Just fill it out, and you can pick up the records tomorrow after three.

questions.stem: How does the man say records are normally delivered through the portal?

options.A: As a printed copy mailed home
options.B: As an official PDF sent by email
options.C: As a fax to the study-abroad office
options.D: As a link texted to the student's phone

questions.explanation: 男士说通过患者门户申请，他们会在三天内发一份官方PDF邮件，故B正确。A错误：他说的是邮件发PDF，不是邮寄纸质件回家，属错误细节。C错误：对话从未提到传真给留学办公室，属虚构方式。D错误：没提到短信发链接，属未提及的细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Wait three days for the portal email
options.B: Ask her program to extend the deadline
options.C: Mail a request to the health center
options.D: Fill out an expedited release form at the desk

questions.explanation: 男士说因为周一就要、时间紧，建议在前台填加急的记录调取表，明天下午就能取，并说马上给她表格，故D正确。A错误：三天太慢赶不上周一，她不会等门户邮件，属被否决的选项。B错误：对话从未提到请项目方延期，属无关下一步。C错误：加急方案是当面填表，不是邮寄申请，属误读细节。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff whether taking fewer credits will affect her grant eligibility

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm thinking of dropping to nine credits next term to lighten my load. Will that affect the grant I'm getting?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It might. Your grant requires you stay enrolled full-time, which is twelve credits. At nine, you'd drop to part-time and the grant could be reduced or cut.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I had no idea it was tied to the credit count.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It is. But before you panic, there's a half-time exception for certain grants. Some still pay a reduced amount at six to eleven credits. It depends on which grant you have.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm honestly not sure which one it is. How do I find out?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Bring me your award letter and I'll look up the exact terms. Don't drop any credits until we've confirmed it, okay?

questions.stem: What is the woman mainly concerned about?

options.A: Whether reducing her credits will affect her grant
options.B: Whether she can graduate a semester early
options.C: Whether her tuition bill has been paid
options.D: Whether she qualifies for a new scholarship

questions.explanation: 女士开门见山问把学分降到九分会不会影响她拿的助学金，全程围绕学分与助学金的关系，故A正确。B错误：她是想'减轻负担'而非提前毕业，属误读情境。C错误：对话从未提到学费账单是否已付，属无关话题。D错误：她关心的是现有助学金会不会受影响，不是申请新奖学金，属无关话题。

questions.stem: What does the man advise the woman to do?

options.A: Immediately drop to nine credits
options.B: Switch to a different grant program
options.C: Bring her award letter before changing credits
options.D: Apply for a part-time student loan

questions.explanation: 男士让她把奖助通知书(award letter)拿来核对具体条款，并叮嘱'确认之前别减任何学分'，故C正确。A错误：他明确说确认前不要减学分，立刻降到九分与建议相反，属被否决的选项。B错误：他说要看她是哪种助学金，没建议换助学金项目，属过度推断。D错误：对话从未提到申请贷款，属无关下一步。

items.situation: Student asks the registrar how to add her preferred name to the class roster so professors stop using her legal name.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm hoping to get my preferred name showing up on the class rosters. Every professor keeps reading out my legal name on the first day, and it's a little awkward.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's totally fixable. We added a preferred-name field to the student portal last year. Have you filled that part in yet?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I put it in my profile, but the rosters still show the old one. Did I miss a step?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The profile and the roster pull from two different places, actually. You have to go to the 'Academic Records' tab and submit a separate display-name request there.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ah, I never opened that tab. How long until the professors see the change?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It syncs overnight, so it'll be updated by tomorrow morning. Just submit it before midnight and you're set for the rest of the term.

questions.stem: Where does the man say the woman must submit her request?

options.A: Under the 'Academic Records' tab
options.B: On her main profile page
options.C: At the registrar's front desk
options.D: Through an email to each professor

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生明确说显示姓名的请求要去'Academic Records'标签里单独提交。B错误：她已经在个人资料里填了，但名册不是从那里读取的，所以不是正确地点。C错误：对话从未提到要去前台办理。D错误：对话没有提到给每位教授发邮件，这是看似合理但未被提及的做法。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Email all of her professors directly
options.B: Wait a full week for the change
options.C: Submit the display-name request before midnight
options.D: Change her legal name on record

questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。男生说在午夜前提交、系统隔夜同步，明早就会更新，所以女生接下来最可能去提交显示姓名请求。A错误：发邮件给教授是无关的步骤，对话里给出的解决方案是portal提交，不是发邮件。B错误：男生说隔夜就更新，根本不用等一周，与对话相反。D错误：她只想改显示名，不是法定姓名，属于过度推断。

items.situation: Student asks library staff how to recall a book that another patron has checked out because she needs it for a paper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, the book I need for my history paper is checked out by someone else. Is there any way I can get it sooner?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You can place a recall on it. That basically forces the current borrower to return it early, usually within a week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: A week? My paper's due in four days, though.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, that's cutting it close. Honestly, you might be better off requesting it through interlibrary loan from the branch campus. They can ship a copy here in two days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's much faster. Where do I put in that request?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just use the 'Request from Partner Library' link on the catalog page. I'd do it today, though, so it arrives in time.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the library desk?

options.A: To pay a fine on an overdue history book
options.B: To get access to a book that is checked out
options.C: To extend the loan period on a book she has
options.D: To ask where the history section is located

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。女生开门见山说她写论文需要的书被别人借走了，问能不能早点拿到，整段对话都围绕如何尽快拿到这本书。A错误：对话从没提到罚款或逾期，是另一个常见图书馆话题。C错误：她不是要续借自己手里的书，而是想拿到别人借走的书，情况正好相反。D错误：她要的是具体某本书，不是问历史区在哪，属于太狭隘的误读。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Place a recall and wait the full week
options.B: Buy her own copy of the book online
options.C: Ask the current borrower to return it
options.D: Request the book through interlibrary loan today

questions.explanation: 正确答案是D。男生算了一下召回要一周来不及，于是建议改用馆际互借从分校调书，两天到，并提醒今天就提交。A错误：男生恰恰指出召回等一周来不及，这是被否决的选项。B错误：网上自己买书在对话里从未提及，属于无关步骤。C错误：直接联系借书人在对话里没有出现，是看似合理但未提到的做法。

items.situation: Student asks housing staff how to report a broken heater in her dorm room before a cold weekend.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, the heater in my room totally stopped working last night, and it's supposed to get really cold this weekend.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh no, that's the worst timing. Let's get a work order in for you right away. What's your room number?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's 412 in Carver Hall. Do I need to be there when they come?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nope, our maintenance crew has a master key. But if you want, you can leave a note on the desk describing the problem.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. How fast do you think someone can come take a look?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Heating issues are top priority, so they'll be there this afternoon. I'll mark it urgent in the system right now.

questions.stem: What is the woman's room number?

options.A: Room 214 in Carver Hall
options.B: Room 421 in Harper Hall
options.C: Room 412 in Carver Hall
options.D: Room 124 in Carver Hall

questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。女生明确说房间是Carver Hall的412。A错误：把房号说成214，数字顺序被改了。B错误：房号和楼名都错了，说成Harper Hall的421。D错误：把412说成124，关键数字被打乱。这三个干扰项都用了对话里出现的数字但篡改了关键事实。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about the repair?

options.A: It will be handled quickly because it's urgent
options.B: It may take several days to schedule
options.C: The woman must be present for the repair
options.D: She should buy a space heater herself

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生说供暖问题是最高优先级，今天下午就会来，还要在系统里标记为紧急，暗示会很快处理。B错误：男生说今天下午就到，不是要等好几天，与对话相反。C错误：男生明确说维修队有万能钥匙，女生不必在场，这是被对话否定的。D错误：自己买取暖器在对话里从没提到，属于无关步骤。

items.situation: Student asks IT staff why she cannot log into the exam-proctoring software the night before a test.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I can't log into the exam-proctoring app. My midterm opens at eight tomorrow and it just keeps saying 'authentication failed.'

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, don't panic. Nine times out of ten, that error means the app hasn't been updated to the latest version. When did you last update it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Um, I installed it back in September and haven't touched it since.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's almost certainly it, then. The proctoring service pushed a security patch last month, and the old version can't connect anymore.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So I just need to reinstall it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Update, not reinstall. Delete the old one, grab the current version from the software portal, and log in with your usual credentials. That should clear it up.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main problem?

options.A: She forgot the password to her exam account
options.B: She cannot log into the proctoring software
options.C: Her midterm exam has been cancelled
options.D: Her laptop will not turn on at all

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。女生一上来就说登不进考试监考软件，一直提示'authentication failed'，整段都在解决这个登录问题。A错误：问题不是忘记密码，而是软件版本过旧，男生最后还让她用平常的账号密码登录，可见密码没问题。C错误：考试被取消在对话里从未提及，是另一个话题。D错误：电脑开不了机也没有出现，属于篡改的细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Email her professor to delay the midterm
options.B: Keep using the old version of the app
options.C: Reset her account password
options.D: Download the latest version from the software portal

questions.explanation: 正确答案是D。男生说要更新而不是重装，删掉旧版、从软件门户下载最新版再用原账号登录，所以女生接下来最可能去门户下载最新版。A错误：给教授发邮件推迟考试在对话里没出现，属于无关步骤。B错误：男生说旧版根本连不上，继续用旧版正是被否决的做法。C错误：重置密码是看似合理但被排除的，男生说用'平常的账号密码'即可，问题不在密码。

items.situation: Student asks dining-hall staff how to flag a nut allergy so the kitchen can prepare safe meals.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just found out I have a pretty serious nut allergy. Is there a way for the kitchen to know that when I come in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Absolutely. We have an allergen profile you can set up. Once it's on file, the staff at each station gets a heads-up when you scan your meal card.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's reassuring. Where do I set up the profile?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You register it at the dining services office in the student center, not online. They'll want a quick note from the health center confirming the allergy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I actually have an appointment there on Thursday, so I'll grab the note then.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Bring it straight to the office and they'll have your profile active the same day.

questions.stem: What does the man say the woman needs to bring?

options.A: A note from the health center about the allergy
options.B: A printed copy of her meal-plan receipt
options.C: A doctor's prescription for medication
options.D: A signed form from her academic advisor

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生说办理过敏档案需要健康中心开一张确认过敏的便条。B错误：餐卡收据在对话里没有提到，属于篡改的细节。C错误：处方药也没有出现，是看似相关但未被提及的东西。D错误：导师签字的表格与过敏登记无关，属于misattribution，把别的流程套到这里。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on Thursday?

options.A: Set up her allergen profile online
options.B: Pick up the confirmation note at her appointment
options.C: Cancel her meal plan entirely
options.D: Eat only at off-campus restaurants

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。女生说周四正好在健康中心有预约，会顺便拿便条，所以周四最可能去取这张确认便条。A错误：男生明确说要去学生中心办公室登记，不是网上，这是被对话否定的。C错误：取消餐卡在对话里从没提到，属于无关步骤。D错误：只在校外吃饭是过度推断，对话只是想让食堂安全备餐，没说放弃食堂。

items.situation: Student asks an academic advisor whether dropping a class now will hurt her financial aid and full-time status.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm thinking about dropping my statistics class, but I'm worried it'll mess up my financial aid. Is that a real concern?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It can be. Aid usually requires full-time status, which here means twelve credits. How many are you carrying right now?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fifteen. Statistics is four of those.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then you're fine on that front. Drop it and you'd still have eleven... wait, no, that puts you at eleven, just under the line.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, so that one credit actually matters a lot.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It really does. If you pick up a one-credit seminar to replace it, you'd stay full-time and keep your aid. I'd sort that out before you drop anything.

questions.stem: What is the woman mainly concerned about?

options.A: Whether her statistics grade will drop
options.B: Whether dropping a class affects her financial aid
options.C: Whether her advisor can change her major
options.D: Whether she can graduate a semester early

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。女生一开始就说想退掉统计课，但担心会影响助学金，整段都在讨论学分和全日制身份对助学金的影响。A错误：她担心的不是成绩高低，而是退课后的助学金，属于篡改的细节。C错误：换专业在对话里从未提及，是另一个话题。D错误：提前一学期毕业也没有出现，属于无关话题。

questions.stem: What does the advisor recommend the woman do?

options.A: Drop statistics immediately to lighten her load
options.B: Switch from financial aid to a student loan
options.C: Accept losing her full-time status
options.D: Add a one-credit seminar before dropping statistics

questions.explanation: 正确答案是D。顾问算出退课后只剩11学分、刚好低于全日制线，于是建议先加一门一学分的研讨课保住全日制再退课。A错误：直接马上退课会掉到11学分、低于全日制线，这正是顾问让她避免的，是被否决的选项。B错误：改用学生贷款在对话里从未提到，属于无关步骤。C错误：接受失去全日制身份与顾问的建议相反，他正是要帮她保住全日制。

items.situation: Student asks bookstore staff how to sell back used textbooks at the end of the term for the best value.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I've got a stack of textbooks I want to sell back. How does that work here?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Easy. Bring them to the buyback counter, we scan the barcodes, and you get cash or store credit on the spot.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Is the cash and the credit the same amount?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good question. Store credit gets you about twenty percent more than the cash offer, so it depends what you'd rather have.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I do need to buy books for next term anyway.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then credit's probably the smarter move for you. Buyback runs through Friday, so don't leave it too late.

questions.stem: What does the man say about store credit?

options.A: It is worth about twenty percent more than cash
options.B: It is worth the same as the cash offer
options.C: It can only be used at the branch campus
options.D: It expires at the end of the week

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生明确说店内credit比现金报价多约20%。B错误：男生说两者并不相等，credit更多，这是被对话否定的篡改细节。C错误：只能在分校用在对话里从未提到，属于看似相关但未提及。D错误：周五截止的是回购活动本身，不是credit过期，把别处的信息错误地套到credit上，属于misattribution。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely choose?

options.A: Store credit, since she'll buy books next term
options.B: Cash, so she can spend it anywhere
options.C: To keep all of her textbooks
options.D: To sell them at a different store

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。女生说反正下学期也要买书，男生也说那选credit更划算，所以她最可能选店内credit。B错误：选现金与她下学期要买书、且credit更多的情形相反，是被否决的方向。C错误：留着所有课本和她一开始就想卖书的意图矛盾，属于相反情形。D错误：去别的店卖在对话里从没提到，属于无关步骤。

items.situation: Student asks gym staff how to sign up for a beginner rock-climbing orientation before using the climbing wall.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd love to try the climbing wall, but I've literally never climbed before. Can I just hop on?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not quite. First-timers have to do a short orientation so we know you can belay and tie in safely. It's only about forty-five minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's not bad. How do I sign up for one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We run them Tuesday and Thursday evenings. You reserve a spot at the front desk or on the rec center app.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I've got the app already. Do I need to bring my own gear?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nope, we lend out shoes and harnesses for the orientation. Just wear something you can move in.

questions.stem: Why can't the woman use the climbing wall right away?

options.A: She must first complete a safety orientation
options.B: She needs to bring her own climbing gear
options.C: The wall is closed for repairs that day
options.D: She has to pay an extra membership fee

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生说新手必须先做一个短的orientation，确认会保护和打结才能上墙。B错误：男生明确说装备由场馆出借，不用自带，这是被对话否定的。C错误：墙因维修关闭在对话里从未提到，属于篡改的细节。D错误：额外会员费也没有出现，属于看似相关但未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy her own harness and shoes
options.B: Reserve an orientation spot on the app
options.C: Climb the wall without any training
options.D: Sign up for a different sport instead

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。女生说自己已经装了rec center的app，男生说在app上就能预约orientation，所以她最可能用app预约。A错误：男生说装备可借，自己买装备属于无关步骤。C错误：不培训直接上墙正是规则不允许、被否决的做法。D错误：改报别的运动在对话里从没出现，属于无关步骤。

items.situation: Student asks health-center staff how to get a flu shot before the campus clinic runs out of doses.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I heard you're giving flu shots this week. Can I still get one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, we've got doses left, but they're going fast. Walk-ins are welcome, though appointments jump the line.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Is there a cost? I'm a little worried about the bill.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's completely free for enrolled students. Just bring your student ID and we'll handle the rest.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, great. When's the best time to come by?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Mornings are quiet. If you book a slot online for tomorrow at nine, you'll barely wait at all.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the cost of the flu shot?

options.A: It is free for enrolled students
options.B: It costs a small co-pay at the desk
options.C: It is billed to the student's insurance
options.D: It is only free with an appointment

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生明确说对在校学生完全免费，带学生证即可。B错误：现场付小额自付费在对话里没出现，属于篡改的细节。C错误：走保险报销也没有提到，属于看似相关但未提及。D错误：把'免费'错误地和'预约'绑在一起，男生说免费对所有在校生，预约只是少排队，属于misattribution。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Book a morning appointment online
options.B: Show up as a walk-in this afternoon
options.C: Wait until next week to decide
options.D: Get the shot at an off-campus pharmacy

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。男生说早上人少，建议明早九点在网上订个时段几乎不用等，女生问的正是什么时候来最好，所以她最可能预约早上的时段。B错误：下午来且走walk-in与男生'早上人少、建议预约'的建议相反，属于被否决的方向。C错误：拖到下周再决定和她担心疫苗发完、想尽快打的情形矛盾，属于相反情形。D错误：去校外药房在对话里从没提到，且校内免费，属于无关步骤。

items.situation: Student asks financial-aid staff why her scholarship disbursement hasn't shown up in her account yet.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my scholarship was supposed to hit my account two weeks ago, but there's still nothing there. Did something go wrong?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check... Okay, the scholarship's approved, but disbursement is on hold pending one missing document.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: A missing document? Nobody told me anything was missing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It looks like the enrollment-verification form never came through. Without it, the system won't release the funds, even though everything else is set.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ugh. So how do I fix it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Submit the form through your aid portal today. Once it's processed, the money should post within three business days.

questions.stem: Why is the woman's scholarship money not in her account?

options.A: Her scholarship application was rejected
options.B: A required document is still missing
options.C: She enrolled in too few credit hours
options.D: The bank reversed the deposit by mistake

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。男生查后说奖学金已批准，但因为缺一份入学验证表，发放被冻结，整段都在解决这份缺失文件。A错误：奖学金其实已批准，不是被拒，与对话相反。C错误：学分不够在对话里从未提到，属于另一个话题。D错误：银行误冲账也没有出现，属于篡改的细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Reapply for the scholarship from scratch
options.B: Wait two more weeks for the deposit
options.C: Call the bank to trace the payment
options.D: Submit the enrollment-verification form online

questions.explanation: 正确答案是D。男生说今天就在助学金portal里提交那份表，处理后三个工作日内款项就会到账，所以女生接下来最可能去提交入学验证表。A错误：奖学金已批准，重新从头申请毫无必要，属于过度推断。B错误：再等两周和她已经等了两周、现在要解决问题的情形相反，是被否决的方向。C错误：打电话给银行查账在对话里从没提到，且问题出在缺文件不在银行，属于无关步骤。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so tired of my playlist. Every song sounds like the same three pop singers.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went through that last semester. What kinds of things have you actually liked recently?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I guess songs with strong storytelling? Like a clear narrator and a few real-life details.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then you should try indie folk. There's a singer named Hana Bryant — she basically writes short stories. Her album from last fall, the one called 'Bus Stop Mornings,' is the place to start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How does it compare to the regular pop stuff?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The arrangements are sparser — usually just guitar, sometimes piano. The whole point is that you focus on the lyrics.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds like exactly what I need. I keep skipping tracks because they all blur together.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then I'd say start with the third track, 'Letters to Iowa.' If that one doesn't pull you in, you can give the rest a miss.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, third track. I'll listen on the bus tonight.

questions.stem: According to the man, what makes Hana Bryant's music different from the pop songs the woman is tired of?

options.A: The arrangements are sparser and meant to focus attention on the lyrics
options.B: The songs are produced entirely with electronic instruments
options.C: The albums are usually shorter than ten minutes
options.D: The singer rarely writes her own material

questions.explanation: 男生明说编曲简洁，多为吉他和钢琴，重点是让你专注歌词。A 正确。B 与原文相反，reversed_situation。C 没有依据，over_inference。D 与男生称她“写短篇小说”相反，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy the entire album on physical CD this evening
options.B: Send Hana Bryant a personal email asking for new music
options.C: Listen to the third track from 'Bus Stop Mornings' on the bus tonight
options.D: Cancel her current pop music subscription completely

questions.explanation: 女生最后明确说今晚在公车上从第三首听起。C 正确。A 没提到买实体专辑，over_inference。B 完全没有这一情节，unrelated_next_step。D 是过度推断，over_inference。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You saw 'Lighthouse Echo' last night, right? What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm torn. The plot twists were really clever — I didn't see the ending coming at all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's what everyone keeps saying online.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: But it was way too long. Almost three hours, and at least forty minutes in the middle could have been cut.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Like a slow stretch in the second act?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. There's this whole subplot about the brother that doesn't go anywhere. By the time the twist arrives, you've already lost some momentum.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did the audience seem bored?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A few people behind me actually got up. Not walked out, but went to get popcorn around the ninety-minute mark.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I was thinking of seeing it tomorrow. Worth it for the twists?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If you go in expecting a long ride, yes. Don't watch it tired.

questions.stem: What is the man's main complaint about the film?

options.A: The acting performances were uneven across the cast
options.B: The film was nearly three hours long, with a slow middle act that should have been cut
options.C: The opening twist was given away too early in the trailer
options.D: The soundtrack distracted from the film's most important scenes

questions.explanation: 男生明说时长接近三小时，中间部分本可以削减四十分钟。B 正确。A、C、D 男生都未提及，分别是 wrong_topic、over_inference、wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What does the man imply when he says "Don't watch it tired"?

options.A: The film should only be watched in a movie theater, not at home
options.B: The woman should plan to see the film with a large group of friends
options.C: The film contains scenes that are unsuitable for evening viewing
options.D: The film's length and slow middle will be harder to enjoy if she is already tired

questions.explanation: 男生暗示电影偏长、中段慢，疲倦时更难坚持。D 正确。A 没提观影方式，over_inference。B 没提到一群朋友，unrelated_next_step。C 是过度联想，over_inference。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish the watercolor class at the community center?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Last week, actually. Six sessions, every Tuesday evening.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Was it worth signing up?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, more than I expected. The instructor focused on three basic skills: mixing colors, controlling water, and layering washes. By session four we were doing small landscapes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you need to bring your own materials?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The first session everything was provided — paper, brushes, paints. From session two on, you had to bring your own paper. Brushes and paints stayed in the room.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was worried about the cost of supplies. How much did the paper come to?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I spent about eighteen dollars at the art store on a pad that lasted the whole course.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's manageable. I think I'll sign up for the next round.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They open registration in two weeks. Don't wait — the evening section fills up fast.

questions.stem: What did students need to bring to the watercolor class starting from the second session?

options.A: A set of brushes and an apron
options.B: A small framed canvas for landscape work
options.C: Their own selection of dry paint pigments
options.D: Their own paper for the class exercises

questions.explanation: 男生说从第二节起需要自带纸张，画笔和颜料留在教室。D 正确。A、C 与原文相反，reversed_situation 或 wrong_detail。B 没有此环节，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Avoid the watercolor class because it requires expensive materials
options.B: Register early when the next round opens because evening sections fill quickly
options.C: Choose a daytime art class because evenings are reserved for advanced students
options.D: Take the class only after she completes a beginner sketching course

questions.explanation: 男生提醒别等，两周后开放报名，晚间段很快报满。B 正确。A 与他对课程的正面态度相反，reversed_situation。C、D 没有这种内容，unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore or quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Can we finally settle the chores and quiet-hours thing? I think we've been winging it for three weeks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, agreed. The dishes have piled up twice already.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking we split it by day. You take dishes Monday and Wednesday, I take Tuesday and Thursday, and we trade Friday weekly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Friday I'm usually out, so weekly trade works if you start. What about the trash?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Whoever's day it isn't, takes it down. That keeps it even.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair. And quiet hours? I have an 8 a.m. class three days a week, so anything past 11 is hard.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Eleven works for me, but I sometimes have group projects that go late. Could I use headphones after eleven if I'm on a call?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, as long as the conversation stays at a low volume. And the desk lamp instead of the overhead, if I'm sleeping.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. I'll write it up and stick it on the fridge.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Make sure the trash rule is in there. That's the one we always forget.

questions.stem: What are the two roommates mainly trying to do?

options.A: Agree on a written schedule for chores and quiet hours
options.B: Decide whether to move into a larger apartment next semester
options.C: Complain to the resident advisor about a noisy neighbor
options.D: Plan a weekend study session for their group project

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕家务分工和安静时段安排，最后女生还说要写下来贴冰箱上。A 正确。B、C、D 都没有出现，都是 wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the roommates handle late-night noise?

options.A: They are unable to compromise and need a third party to mediate
options.B: They will rely on the resident advisor to enforce a strict 11 p.m. cutoff
options.C: They agree to flexibility past 11 p.m. as long as headphones and low-volume conversation are used
options.D: They have decided to ban all late-night calls or group work entirely

questions.explanation: 男生同意女生 11 点后可以用耳机低声通话，作为安静时段的灵活安排。C 正确。A、B、D 都与对话中达成的妥协相反，reversed_situation。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's your urban-sketching elective going? I'm thinking of taking it next semester.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it's been harder than I expected. The instructor pushes us to draw outside in twenty-minute bursts, and weather wrecks half the sessions.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh wow. Do you actually finish anything?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sometimes. But what surprised me is that the time pressure helps. Once you stop trying to be perfect, you actually start seeing things.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds kind of inspiring, actually. Maybe I'll sign up after all.

questions.stem: Why does the woman approach the man?

options.A: To ask about his experience in an urban-sketching class
options.B: To borrow his sketching supplies for the weekend
options.C: To complain about the weather on campus
options.D: To recommend a different art elective to him

questions.explanation: 女生开场就问 "How's your urban-sketching elective going?"，表示她在考虑下学期选这门课。其他选项与对话开头主题无关。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about working under time pressure?

options.A: It made him quit the elective early.
options.B: It mainly causes him to make more mistakes.
options.C: It has unexpectedly improved how he observes scenes.
options.D: It only works when the weather is perfect.

questions.explanation: 男生说当你不再追求完美时，开始 "start seeing things"，暗示时间压力反而改善了他观察的方式。其他选项与他的语气或事实相反。

items.situation: two friends recommending a podcast or TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that science podcast I mentioned, the one about everyday physics?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not yet. I've been hooked on a history series about ancient libraries. Episodes are about thirty minutes each.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds great! Where did you find it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's actually on the campus library's free streaming portal. You just log in with your student ID.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? I didn't even know we had that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's pretty hidden. I'll send you the link tonight.

questions.stem: Where can the man's history series be accessed?

options.A: Through a paid podcast subscription
options.B: Through the campus library's free streaming portal
options.C: Through a commercial radio app
options.D: Through the science department's website

questions.explanation: 男生明确说节目在校园图书馆的免费流媒体平台上，用学生 ID 登录即可。其他来源与他的描述不符。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Cancel her existing podcast subscriptions
options.B: Recommend her physics podcast to the library
options.C: Wait for the man to send her the link
options.D: Look for the series through a paid streaming service

questions.explanation: 男生说今晚会把链接发给她，她最自然的下一步就是等他发链接。其他选项要么是对话未提及的行为，要么与谈话方向相反。

items.situation: two students talking about a pet or houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's that giant fern you adopted last month?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's actually thriving, surprisingly. I moved it next to my desk lamp and the new leaves came in within two weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I thought ferns hated bright light.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I thought so too. But this one's tropical, so it likes the warmth more than the brightness, I guess.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh, interesting. Mine keeps dropping leaves. Maybe I'm overwatering it.

questions.stem: What did the man do that helped his fern grow?

options.A: He moved it outside to a sunny balcony.
options.B: He repotted it in larger drainage soil.
options.C: He placed it near his desk lamp.
options.D: He started fertilizing it weekly.

questions.explanation: 男生说他把蕨类植物挪到 desk lamp 旁边，两周内长出新叶。其他选项是对话中未出现的具体动作。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's fern?

options.A: It may be suffering from too much watering.
options.B: It is the same tropical species as the man's.
options.C: It has already died this semester.
options.D: It was originally a gift from the man.

questions.explanation: 女生说自己的蕨类在掉叶子，并猜测可能是浇水过多，所以最可能的推断就是 overwatering 是问题来源。其他选项无依据。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw that surprised them

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I caught that documentary about urban farming last night. It honestly changed how I think about food.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? I'd assumed it was going to be more political.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I expected that too! But it focused on individual rooftop farmers in three different cities. Each story was super personal.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That sounds way better than the trailer suggested.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. The trailer made it look preachy, but the actual film is mostly conversations.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll definitely give it a watch this weekend, then.

questions.stem: Why did the woman find the documentary effective?

options.A: It featured well-known political activists in detail.
options.B: It centered on personal stories of individual rooftop farmers.
options.C: It compared rooftop farming to traditional rural farming directly.
options.D: It included sweeping landscape footage of the countryside.

questions.explanation: 女生说纪录片聚焦三座城市的个别屋顶农夫的个人故事，所以这才是她觉得有效的原因。其他选项与她描述相反或无据。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the documentary's trailer?

options.A: It did not accurately represent the film's tone.
options.B: It was banned by several streaming services.
options.C: It was longer than the film itself.
options.D: It featured the same farmers as the documentary.

questions.explanation: 女生说 trailer 让纪录片看上去 preachy，但实际内容主要是对话，暗示预告片误导了观众对影片基调的判断。其他选项无依据。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking or beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are we still on for the Cedar Ridge hike Saturday morning?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, definitely. But weather says rain after one, so we should aim for an early start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. I can pick you up at six-thirty if that works.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six-thirty's perfect. Could you also bring an extra water bottle? Mine cracked last week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure, no problem. Anything else?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just snacks, maybe. I'll bring the trail map.

questions.stem: What time will the woman pick up the man on Saturday?
questions.explanation: 女生提议六点半接他，男生说 perfect。其他时间或来自其他细节，或与对话事实不符。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's preparation?

options.A: He will replace his cracked water bottle himself.
options.B: He plans to navigate the hike using the trail map.
options.C: He will not need any snacks during the hike.
options.D: He intends to cancel the trip if it rains.

questions.explanation: 男生说自己会带 trail map，暗示他会用它在路上导航。其他选项与他的话相反或没有依据。

items.situation: two friends planning a birthday surprise for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are we still doing something for Daniela's birthday on Thursday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I was thinking we could do a surprise at the study lounge after her seminar.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a cute idea, but the lounge gets really loud around five. People are leaving the library. It'd ruin the moment.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, fair point. What if we did the kitchenette in our dorm instead? It's quieter and there's a fridge for the cake.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Better. But Daniela never goes to your dorm. She'd be suspicious if you randomly invited her.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. Okay, here's a thought — what if you ask her to come help you with the chemistry homework, and I just show up with the cake when she gets there?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's perfect, actually. She offered to help me last week, so it won't seem weird.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. I'll handle the cake and decorations. Can you message everyone else and tell them to be at your room by four-thirty?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure. And I'll text Daniela tonight about the homework so she's expecting it.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to organize a surprise birthday celebration for their friend
options.B: How to finish their chemistry homework before Thursday evening
options.C: How to find a quiet place to study during the busy week
options.D: How to convince Daniela to move out of her current dorm

questions.explanation: 对话主要讨论如何为 Daniela 安排惊喜生日派对，A 正确。B 把化学作业当成主题（其实只是借口）；C 把安静地点过度概括；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: She will go pick up the birthday cake from the bakery
options.B: She will go finish her chemistry homework before the seminar
options.C: She will text Daniela about meeting to do homework together
options.D: She will go decorate her dorm room before everyone arrives

questions.explanation: 女生最后说她今晚会给 Daniela 发消息约一起做作业，C 正确。A 是男生负责；B 不是即将的行动；D 与对话中分工不符。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's the new apartment? You moved off campus this semester, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's been great except for the commute. I'm trying to figure out the best way to get to my morning class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How are you getting there now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I've been taking the bus. It's reliable, but it takes thirty-five minutes because of all the stops.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you thought about biking? I bike every day. It's only twenty minutes if you take the riverside trail.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I don't have a bike, and honestly, on rainy days that sounds miserable.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fair. The bus is probably your best bet for now, then. Walking would take forever from your place — at least forty-five minutes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I tried walking once. Never again.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You could rent one of those campus share bikes by the month. Then on nice days, you switch to biking, and you keep the bus pass for bad weather.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's actually a smart compromise. I'll look into it.

questions.stem: How long does it take the man to reach campus by bus?

options.A: About twenty minutes each morning
options.B: About thirty-five minutes each morning
options.C: About forty-five minutes each morning
options.D: About one full hour each morning

questions.explanation: 男生说坐巴士要 35 分钟，B 正确。A 是女生骑车的时间；C 是步行时间；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: He will sell his new bus pass to a different student
options.B: He will move back to his old on-campus dormitory soon
options.C: He will buy a brand-new bike and a rain jacket today
options.D: He will look into renting a monthly campus share bike

questions.explanation: 男生最后说会去了解共享单车月租，D 正确。A 与对话无关；B 与对话相反；C 把买车与租车混淆，男生明确说不想买车。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, so for the day trip to Riverton on Saturday, I'm looking at the transit schedule and it's confusing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Why? What's the issue?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Well, the train leaves at seven-fifteen, which is great, but it only runs to the old Riverton station, which is forty-five minutes from the actual downtown.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh. And the bus?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The bus goes straight to downtown but doesn't leave until nine-thirty. By the time we arrive, it'll be after eleven.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That cuts our day short. The galleries close at six.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. There's also a ride-share with another student club, but you have to commit by Wednesday and they're full.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Already? Okay. What if we just took the train and grabbed a taxi from the old station?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I checked. Taxis from there are expensive — like forty dollars each. That's almost double the train ticket.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. You know what, the train is fine. Let's just split the taxi and treat ourselves. We'll still have most of the day for the galleries.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, okay. Splitting it makes it manageable.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Which gallery in Riverton they should visit first on Saturday
options.B: Whether to join a student club that runs a ride-share program
options.C: How to choose a transportation option for their Saturday day trip
options.D: Why the train schedule changed recently for the Riverton route

questions.explanation: 他们主要讨论如何选择周六的交通方式，C 正确。A 是旁支细节；B 只是被排除的选项之一；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do for the trip?

options.A: They will take the early train and share a taxi together from the station
options.B: They will leave on the nine-thirty bus and cut their gallery visit short
options.C: They will wait for a spot to open up in the student club ride-share
options.D: They will cancel the trip and reschedule for the following weekend

questions.explanation: 男生提议坐火车再合租出租车，女生同意分摊费用，A 正确。B 与最终决定不符；C 已被排除（满了）；D 没有讨论取消。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Friday's dinner party, I'm doing a cheese board. What should I get?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely something soft, like brie. And then a harder one — sharp cheddar or maybe gouda.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good. I was thinking three cheeses total. What's the third one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Something blue, if people are into it. But blue cheese is risky — not everyone likes it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, maybe skip it. What about a goat cheese instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's safer. People generally like goat cheese. Get the kind with herbs on the outside.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. What else? I need crackers, obviously.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Crackers, and grapes. Grapes are essential. And get some honey for the goat cheese — trust me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honey, really? Okay, I'll trust you on that. What about meat? Should I add prosciutto?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, but only a small package. People go for the cheese first.

questions.stem: What does the man specifically recommend buying for the goat cheese?

options.A: A small jar of honey to serve with it
options.B: A wedge of imported gouda for the pairing
options.C: A package of dried fruit and salted nuts
options.D: A bottle of olive oil to brush on top

questions.explanation: 男生明确建议买蜂蜜搭配山羊奶酪，A 正确。B 把另一种奶酪误置；C 与对话无关；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about why the speakers decided against blue cheese?

options.A: Blue cheese was unavailable at the local grocery store
options.B: Blue cheese might not appeal to all of their guests
options.C: Blue cheese was too expensive for the small party budget
options.D: Blue cheese spoils more quickly than the other selections

questions.explanation: 男生说蓝奶酪有风险，并非人人喜欢，所以推断它可能不合所有客人口味，B 正确。A 与对话无关；C 与对话无关；D 与对话无关。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I haven't seen you all week. How's the new job going?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it's been an adjustment. I started that part-time job at the bottling plant on Monday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The bottling plant? I didn't think you'd take something like that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, well, the pay is much better than the campus jobs. Sixteen an hour, plus a meal voucher each shift.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow, that's good. But what about the schedule? Doesn't it conflict with your classes?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the part I'm still figuring out. They want me on the four-to-eight shift Monday through Thursday. My Tuesday lab usually runs until five.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oof. Can you switch labs?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I emailed the professor about moving to the Wednesday section. She hasn't replied yet, but I'm hoping she will.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: If not, can you switch shifts at work? Like, do Friday instead of Tuesday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Maybe. I haven't asked. I was waiting to hear back from the professor first.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How the man is adjusting to his new part-time factory job
options.B: How the man should choose between two competing internships
options.C: Why the campus jobs typically pay less than off-campus jobs
options.D: How the woman could find part-time work at the same plant

questions.explanation: 对话主要讨论男生新工作的适应情况，特别是与课程的冲突，A 正确。B 偏离主题；C 把片段细节当主题；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next regarding the schedule conflict?

options.A: He will quit the new job and apply to campus positions
options.B: He will wait for the professor's reply before contacting his manager
options.C: He will immediately swap his Tuesday shift to a Friday slot
options.D: He will drop the Tuesday lab and retake it next semester

questions.explanation: 男生说在听到教授回复前不打算和工厂谈，B 正确。A 与对话无关；C 与他说的“先等教授”相反；D 与对话无关。

items.situation: two friends debating whether to buy concert tickets early or wait for last-minute deals

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you see the concert at the Riverside Arena got announced? I'm thinking of grabbing tickets tonight.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Already? The show isn't until June. Last-minute deals usually pop up the week before.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, but the good seats sell out fast. My cousin waited last time and ended up in the back row.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, that's a good point. Maybe split the difference — buy now if presale opens, otherwise wait.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. I'll set an alert and check Friday morning.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: When to purchase tickets for the concert
options.B: Where to sit at the Riverside Arena
options.C: Whether to attend the concert in June
options.D: How to find discount tickets online

questions.explanation: 对话主要围绕“现在买票还是等到临场再买”展开，因此他们讨论的核心是何时购票。B 只是顺带提到“好座位会被抢光”，并非主要话题；C 他们都没质疑要不要去；D 也没有讨论网上折扣。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on Friday morning?

options.A: Wait until late June to purchase her tickets
options.B: Travel to Riverside Arena to buy paper tickets
options.C: Check whether early presale tickets are available
options.D: Ask her cousin which seats to buy first

questions.explanation: 男生建议“presale 一开就买，否则再等”，女生回应“好主意，我周五早上看看”，所以她最可能在周五查看是否开了预售。A 是她原本想避免的“等等再买”；B 没有讨论亲自去现场；D 她只是提到表姐的经历，没说要请教她。

items.situation: two students discussing a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new place, Maple Spoon, downtown yet?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not yet. I heard the noodle bowls are amazing but the line at lunch is brutal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Skip the lunch rush. Go around three in the afternoon. I had the spicy peanut noodles and they were unreal — best meal I've had all month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sold. Was anything overpriced or just kind of average?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the desserts. They look pretty but taste flat. Get the noodles, then grab coffee somewhere else.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good tip. I'll head over this Sunday.

questions.stem: Which dish does the woman recommend?

options.A: The mushroom dumplings on the appetizer menu
options.B: The spicy peanut noodles she ordered there
options.C: The seasonal dessert with a maple sauce
options.D: The grilled fish bowl with brown rice

questions.explanation: 女生明确说她点的“spicy peanut noodles”是这个月最好的一餐，所以推荐这道菜。A、D 完全没提及；C 与对话相反，她说甜点中看不中吃。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's overall opinion of Maple Spoon?

options.A: She thinks the restaurant is overpriced and rarely worth visiting
options.B: She believes the lunch hour is the best time to dine there
options.C: She regrets recommending the place to her classmate
options.D: She considers the main dishes better than the desserts

questions.explanation: 她大力推荐面条但批评甜点“taste flat”，因此她认为主菜比甜点好。A 她只批评甜点，不是整家店；B 她建议避开午餐高峰；C 她主动推荐并未表现后悔。

items.situation: two students at the rec center talking about a free intro yoga class

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hey, did you sign up for that free intro yoga class at the rec center yet?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I tried this morning, but the website said the session was full.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? They added a second slot on Thursday at 7:00 PM. Check again.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, nice. Do I need to bring my own mat?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, no rentals this time. There's a sign-up form at the front desk too if the website acts up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll swing by after my chem lab.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about Thursday?

options.A: A new yoga instructor will lead the class
options.B: A registration fee was added that morning
options.C: A second class session was opened that day
options.D: A mat rental program was launched recently

questions.explanation: 男生说“他们周四晚上 7:00 加了第二场”，所以那天新开了一节课。A、B、D 在对话中均未出现。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Visit the rec center after her chemistry lab
options.B: Buy a yoga mat at the campus bookstore
options.C: Email the front desk to request a refund
options.D: Wait until the morning session reopens

questions.explanation: 女生最后说“化学实验课后我顺路过去”，且男生提到前台也能报名，所以她最可能化学课后去 rec center 报名。B 她从未说要新买垫子；C 没有退款话题；D 上午的课她说不上。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you figure out how we're getting to Riverton on Saturday? I checked the bus schedule and the express only runs at seven in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Seven? Yikes, that's brutal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I know. But if we take the regular bus, it stops everywhere and gets there around noon. We'd barely have any time at the festival.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, what about the train? My roommate said there's a new weekend train that leaves at nine.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? That sounds way better. Is it expensive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's twelve dollars one way with a student ID. I'll book the tickets tonight through the rail app.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect, I'll pay you back tomorrow.

questions.stem: What is the problem with the express bus?

options.A: It departs very early in the morning
options.B: It costs more than the train ticket
options.C: It does not run on Saturdays
options.D: It is fully booked for the weekend

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生明确说快车只在早上七点发车，男生反应说"太残酷了"，说明问题在于出发时间太早。B错误：对话从未比较快车和火车的价格。C错误：女生说的是发车时间，没说不在周六运营。D错误：对话中没有提到座位售罄的问题。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Take the regular bus to Riverton
options.B: Ask his roommate for a ride to the station
options.C: Reserve train tickets through a mobile app
options.D: Cancel the trip to the festival

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生明确说"今晚我会通过铁路应用订票"，因此他最可能做的就是用手机应用订火车票。A错误：男生和女生已决定不坐公交，普通公交是被否决的选项。B错误：对话中虽然提到室友，但只是室友告诉他有这趟火车，不涉及让室友送他去车站。D错误：他们正在积极规划去节日，没有任何取消行程的迹象。

items.situation: two students talking about a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So you finally tried Beacon Street? I've been hearing nonstop hype about it. Was it actually worth the wait?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the atmosphere's gorgeous, but the menu is hit or miss. The pasta everyone raves about online? Pretty average.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. So what should I order if I go?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Skip the pasta and go straight for the duck confit. It's the only dish the chef actually trained on, and you can tell.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Duck? I usually wouldn't pick that, but I trust you.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me, it's the move. Oh, and the desserts come from a bakery next door, so don't waste room on those.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good to know. I'll grab dessert somewhere else after.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's a little gelato shop two blocks over that's incredible.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the conversation?

options.A: To complain about the high prices at a new restaurant
options.B: To share advice about what to order at a restaurant
options.C: To decide whether to eat downtown this weekend
options.D: To recommend a bakery and gelato shop nearby

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：整段对话围绕男生告诉女生在这家新餐厅应该点什么菜（鸭肉而不是意面）以及避开什么（甜点）。A错误：对话从未提到价格高的问题。C错误：女生已经决定要去，不是在犹豫是否去市中心吃饭。D错误：甜点店和冰淇淋店只是次要细节，不是对话主旨。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's plan after dinner?

options.A: She will return to Beacon Street another night
options.B: She will order the pasta despite the man's warning
options.C: She will write an online review of the restaurant
options.D: She will buy dessert at a different location

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生明确说"我会在别的地方买甜点"，男生还推荐了两个街区外的冰淇淋店，所以她饭后会去别的地方买甜点。A错误：对话中没有任何关于她再次光顾Beacon Street的暗示，是过度推理。B错误：她说"我信你"，表示会听男生的建议，意面是被否决的选项。C错误：对话中没有提到写评论，与对话无关。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that thriller you've been talking about. The Quiet Hour.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the twists were brilliant. I didn't see the ending coming at all, and the second one in the middle? Total shock.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right? That's why I keep recommending it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: But, wow, it's long. Almost three hours. There's a whole subplot with the sister that they could've cut entirely.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'll admit that part dragged. I actually checked my watch twice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same. I might rewatch it sometime, but I'd skip ahead through the sister scenes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's actually a fan edit online that trims about forty minutes. I'll send you the link.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, please do!

questions.stem: What specific part of the movie did the woman think should have been cut?

options.A: The opening sequence of the film
options.B: The final twist near the end
options.C: A subplot involving a sister character
options.D: A long flashback in the middle

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生明确说"姐姐那条副线他们完全可以剪掉"，所以她认为应该删除涉及姐姐这个角色的副线。A错误：对话从未提到开场镜头有问题。B错误：女生其实非常赞美结尾的反转，认为它出人意料。D错误：对话中没有提到中段的闪回情节，这是听起来合理但未提及的细节。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after the conversation?

options.A: Send the woman a link to a shorter fan edit
options.B: Rewatch the movie with the woman this weekend
options.C: Write a negative review about the movie's length
options.D: Stop recommending the movie to other friends

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我会把链接发给你"，指的是网上一个删减了约四十分钟的影迷版本。B错误：对话中没有提到他们要一起重看这部电影，是过度推理。C错误：男生承认有部分拖沓，但他仍然在推荐这部电影，写差评违背他的态度。D错误：他明显仍然欣赏这部电影并继续向他人推荐，与对话信息相反。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, the activities fair ended yesterday and I still can't decide. Should I sign up for the photography club or the intramural soccer team?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, both meet on Tuesdays, right? You can't really do both.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. And I already have my chemistry lab on Thursday nights, so I need to be careful.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, what are you actually hoping to get out of it? Like, friends? Exercise? A new skill?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I want to meet people outside my major. The chem crowd's nice but kind of intense.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then soccer, hands down. The intramural league is super social, and they go out together every weekend.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Even if I'm not great at soccer?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Totally. Half the team had never played before this year. It's all about showing up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'm convinced. I'll register tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to balance lab work with social activities
options.B: Whether photography club fees are worth paying
options.C: The best way to make friends outside one's major
options.D: Which extracurricular activity the woman should join

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：整段对话围绕女生在摄影社和校内足球队之间犹豫不决，男生帮她做选择。A错误：化学实验只是被简单提及作为时间冲突，不是讨论的主题。B错误：对话中完全没有提到会费问题。C错误：交友是她选择活动的动机之一，但这是太狭窄的视角，主题是选哪个活动。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's soccer ability?

options.A: She played competitively in high school
options.B: She has limited experience with the sport
options.C: She refuses to join because of her skill level
options.D: She is the team's most talented new member

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生问"即使我足球踢得不好也可以吗？"暗示她经验有限，所以可以推断她对这项运动经验不多。A错误：她担心自己技术不好，与高中竞技踢球矛盾，与对话信息相反。C错误：男生说不用紧张后她答应报名，她并没有拒绝加入，被否决的选项。D错误：她担心自己不擅长，称她是队里最有天赋的新成员是过度推理。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, didn't you take that weekend pottery class at the community center last month? How was it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I loved it. Way more relaxing than I expected. The instructor was really patient.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking about signing up. What did you actually make?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We started with simple bowls the first weekend, then moved on to mugs. Mine came out a little crooked, but it works.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Aww, that's so cute. Was it expensive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sixty bucks for four sessions, and that includes all the clay and glazes. Pretty good deal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's reasonable. Do I need to bring anything?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just wear old clothes. Trust me, you'll get clay everywhere.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll register this afternoon.

questions.stem: What was the first item the man made in the pottery class?

options.A: A simple bowl
options.B: A crooked coffee mug
options.C: A glazed dinner plate
options.D: A small clay vase

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我们第一个周末从简单的碗开始"，所以他做的第一件东西是简单的碗。B错误：杯子是第二个周末做的，不是第一件，是混淆细节。C错误：对话中没有提到盘子，听起来合理但未提及。D错误：对话中没有提到花瓶，听起来合理但未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do this afternoon?

options.A: Go shopping for new clothes to wear
options.B: Ask the man to attend the class with her
options.C: Sign up for the pottery class herself
options.D: Visit the community center to see the studio

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生在对话最后明确说"我下午就去报名"，因此她下午最可能做的事是给陶艺课报名。A错误：男生让她穿旧衣服，而不是去买新衣服，与男生的建议相反。B错误：对话中没有提到邀请男生一起上课，与对话无关。D错误：她说要去报名，没说要先去社区中心实地参观，是过度推理。

items.situation: two students debating which library floor is best for studying

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, where do you usually study now? Third floor or basement?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the third floor's gotten too noisy. They added group tables.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, really? I haven't been since last spring.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try the basement. It's a silent zone, and there are private carrels by the windows.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Carrels by windows? That sounds perfect for my thesis work.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just grab one before two. They fill up fast after that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll head there tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: What does the man say has changed on the third floor?

options.A: It has become noisier than before
options.B: It has added new vending machines
options.C: It has limited hours for finals
options.D: It has lost its window seating area

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说三楼变得太吵，因为加了小组讨论桌。B错误：对话从未提到自动售货机。C错误：对话没提期末考试期间时间受限。D错误：对话只提到地下室有窗户旁的隔间，没说三楼失去窗座。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Move her thesis work back upstairs as before
options.B: Reserve a group table for her thesis discussion
options.C: Head to the basement to find a private carrel
options.D: Continue studying in her dorm room as usual

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生最后说"明天早上我会去那里"，结合男生让她在两点前到地下室抢隔间，可推断她会去地下室找隔间。A错误：三楼正是被否决的选项，她不会回去。B错误：小组桌正是导致三楼吵闹的原因，她不会去预订。D错误：对话中没有讨论待在宿舍的方案。

items.situation: two friends planning to split a textbook cost for a chemistry course

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you see how much the chemistry textbook costs this semester? Almost two hundred dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yikes. I was thinking we could split a used copy and share notes through the term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, that could work. We have the same lab section anyway, so we'd be on the same pace.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, and the library has one on reserve too, in case we both need it the same night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good backup plan. Let's check the bookstore's used section after lunch today.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sounds great. I'll bring my student ID for the discount.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main suggestion to the man?

options.A: Buy a brand-new copy from the campus bookstore directly
options.B: Share a used textbook and split the cost
options.C: Borrow detailed notes from a previous classmate
options.D: Sign up for a totally different lab section

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生明确提出"我们可以合买一本二手书然后共享笔记"。A错误：对话中没有提到全新书，反而是买二手。C错误：对话从未提及向以前的同学借笔记。D错误：他们已经在同一个实验班，不需要换班。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library copy of the textbook?

options.A: It is currently checked out by another student all term
options.B: It can be borrowed for the whole semester at once
options.C: It is missing several important chapters in the book
options.D: It serves as a backup when their copy is taken

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生提到图书馆有一本作为备用，"以防我们两个同一晚都需要它"，所以图书馆的书是他们共享本不可用时的备用方案。A错误：对话没说图书馆的书被借走。B错误：对话没提整学期可借。C错误：对话从未提到缺章。

items.situation: two roommates deciding how to decorate their dorm for the holidays

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, are we doing anything for the dorm before break? Last year was so plain.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I was thinking some string lights and a small fake tree from the dollar store.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's cute. But weren't string lights banned in dorms because of the wiring issue?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, you're right. The RA said battery-operated ones are okay though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll grab a set at the campus store tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: And I'll figure out the tree situation this weekend.

questions.stem: What does the man originally suggest buying?

options.A: A large fabric wreath for the dorm door
options.B: A pair of decorative pillows from the bookstore
options.C: A string of lights and a small tree
options.D: A scented candle set for the study desks

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说"我在想灯串和一棵小假树"，所以最初的建议就是灯串和小树。A错误：对话没提花环。B错误：男生提的是dollar store，不是bookstore，且没提枕头。D错误：对话从未提到香薰蜡烛。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about plug-in string lights?

options.A: They are not permitted in the dorm rooms
options.B: They are sold only at off-campus stores
options.C: They are too expensive for most students
options.D: They are difficult to install on dorm walls

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生提到灯串因为线路问题在宿舍被禁，男生确认"你说得对，电池式的才被允许"，可推断插电式灯串不被允许。B错误：对话没提销售地点。C错误：价格从未讨论。D错误：安装难度未涉及。

items.situation: a student asking a friend for recommendations on a new couch for the dorm

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, you got that couch for your common room last semester, right? Where did you end up finding it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the secondhand one. I picked it up from that warehouse on Elm Street. They had a bunch of options under three hundred dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Three hundred is still kind of steep for me. My roommates and I are splitting it four ways, so we want to keep it under two hundred total.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: In that case, honestly, just check the student housing forum. People dump furniture at the end of every semester. Last May I saw a leather one going for fifty bucks because the owner was moving out the next day.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, really? Fifty? That's amazing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah. The trick is checking around finals week. Just be ready to pick it up the same day, because they usually want it gone immediately.

questions.stem: According to the conversation, what is the woman's budget constraint?

options.A: Two hundred dollars total, split four ways
options.B: Three hundred dollars for a single buyer
options.C: Fifty dollars maximum from the warehouse
options.D: Whatever the leather couch on the forum costs

questions.explanation: 女生说四人合资，总预算两百以内。B 是男生提到的另一参考价，C 是男生提到的特价例子，D 是混淆。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about successfully finding a cheap couch on the forum?

options.A: Buyers must register with the Elm Street warehouse first
options.B: Buyers must pay the original retail price plus delivery
options.C: Buyers must be ready to pick up the same day they see the listing
options.D: Buyers must wait until the beginning of a semester

questions.explanation: 男生强调要当天搬走，因为卖家急着清空。A 与讲述无关，B 与便宜价格相反，D 时间反了。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So I tried that new place on Birch Street yesterday — Loom Coffee. Have you been?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went once last week. The latte was good, but seven dollars feels like a lot for what's basically a regular drink.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I actually thought the matcha was worth it. It comes with a tiny shortbread cookie and you can sit there with your laptop forever — nobody asks you to leave.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, fair. The seating situation is way better than the cafe by the library. I'll give them that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? And they have those long communal tables with actual outlets. The library cafe charges almost the same but you have to fight for a power strip.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hm. Yeah, maybe I'm judging it on the wrong thing. I was just looking at the drink price by itself.

questions.stem: According to the conversation, what is the man's initial complaint about Loom Coffee?

options.A: The matcha is too sweet for his taste
options.B: The seating is uncomfortable for studying
options.C: The latte is too expensive at seven dollars
options.D: The cookies are stale by the afternoon

questions.explanation: 男生最初的不满是拿铁七元太贵。其他都是错误描述。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's view by the end of the conversation?

options.A: He has decided never to visit Loom Coffee again
options.B: He plans to write a critical review online
options.C: He prefers the library cafe for its quieter atmosphere
options.D: He realizes he was judging the cafe on price alone

questions.explanation: 男生最后承认只看价格判断有偏差。A 与对话相反，B 没提，C 与对比内容相反。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally finished that watercolor class at the community center. Six weeks, every Saturday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, the one you signed up for in February? I was thinking about doing the spring session.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, it was harder than I expected. The instructor was great, but I thought I'd be making pretty landscapes and instead we spent four weeks just on color mixing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wait, four weeks on mixing? That sounds tedious.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It was, at first. But by week five I realized why she made us do it. When we finally did landscapes, I actually knew how to get the colors I wanted. People in my class who skipped the early weeks were just guessing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. That actually makes me less worried about the spring session. I'd rather build a foundation than just produce something that looks okay but I can't replicate.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: The cost of art classes at the community center
options.B: Whether community art classes accept student discounts
options.C: The value of the watercolor class's slow technical foundation
options.D: The instructor's qualifications and previous teaching record

questions.explanation: 对话主要谈这门水彩课慢慢打基础的价值。其他都是分散注意。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's likely decision regarding the spring session?

options.A: He is more inclined to enroll after hearing about the foundation
options.B: He has decided to switch to a different art form entirely
options.C: He is now hesitant because of the time commitment
options.D: He has already paid for the session and cannot cancel

questions.explanation: 男生最后说现在不那么担心，更倾向报名。其他都与对话相反或无关。

items.situation: two students talking about which textbook edition to buy for chemistry

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm trying to decide between the new tenth edition of the chemistry textbook and the older ninth edition. The new one is one-fifty, the old one is twenty bucks used.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: How different can they really be? Chemistry hasn't changed that much.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's what I thought. But Professor Castellano specifically said the practice problems are renumbered in the new edition, and homework references the tenth edition's problem numbers.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. That's a real problem. You'd have to map problems back and forth every week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. My friend tried that last semester for organic chem and gave up by week four. She ended up borrowing the new edition from the library reserves every Sunday night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wait, the library has reserves? That might be the answer. You'd save the money and still have access to the right problem numbers.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Why Professor Castellano changed editions this semester
options.B: Whether organic chemistry has become harder over time
options.C: Why the textbook publisher renumbered the problems
options.D: Whether to buy the new chemistry textbook or use a cheaper alternative

questions.explanation: 对话核心是是否买新书或用便宜方案。其他都是支撑性细节或不相关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's suggestion regarding library reserves?

options.A: He thinks the woman should buy the new edition just in case
options.B: He thinks reserves can substitute for owning the new edition
options.C: He thinks the library has stopped offering reserves this year
options.D: He thinks the older edition is academically superior

questions.explanation: 男生提议用图书馆储备本省钱又有正确题号。其他都是相反或不相关。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before an exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Our linguistics midterm is on Thursday. Want to study together Tuesday night?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Tuesday is tough. I have my phonetics lab until eight. We could meet after, but I'd be pretty fried.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Wednesday is too late — I want at least one full day to review on my own after.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: What about Tuesday morning? I'm free from ten until my lab at two.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that actually works. I was thinking of evening because the library group rooms get crowded, but the morning slot is usually wide open.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. And we can grab the corner room with the whiteboard — that one's perfect for the syntax tree diagrams we'll need to practice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. Tuesday at ten, the room with the whiteboard. I'll bring the practice tree problems.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to do?

options.A: Schedule a joint study session before their linguistics midterm
options.B: Decide whether to attend the phonetics lab on Tuesday
options.C: Plan a long-term study group for the entire semester
