conversation.text: Honestly, the atmosphere's gorgeous, but the menu is hit or miss. The pasta everyone raves about online? Pretty average.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. So what should I order if I go?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Skip the pasta and go straight for the duck confit. It's the only dish the chef actually trained on, and you can tell.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Duck? I usually wouldn't pick that, but I trust you.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me, it's the move. Oh, and the desserts come from a bakery next door, so don't waste room on those.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good to know. I'll grab dessert somewhere else after.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's a little gelato shop two blocks over that's incredible.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the conversation?

options.A: To complain about the high prices at a new restaurant
options.B: To share advice about what to order at a restaurant
options.C: To decide whether to eat downtown this weekend
options.D: To recommend a bakery and gelato shop nearby

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：整段对话围绕男生告诉女生在这家新餐厅应该点什么菜（鸭肉而不是意面）以及避开什么（甜点）。A错误：对话从未提到价格高的问题。C错误：女生已经决定要去，不是在犹豫是否去市中心吃饭。D错误：甜点店和冰淇淋店只是次要细节，不是对话主旨。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's plan after dinner?

options.A: She will return to Beacon Street another night
options.B: She will order the pasta despite the man's warning
options.C: She will write an online review of the restaurant
options.D: She will buy dessert at a different location

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生明确说"我会在别的地方买甜点"，男生还推荐了两个街区外的冰淇淋店，所以她饭后会去别的地方买甜点。A错误：对话中没有任何关于她再次光顾Beacon Street的暗示，是过度推理。B错误：她说"我信你"，表示会听男生的建议，意面是被否决的选项。C错误：对话中没有提到写评论，与对话无关。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that thriller you've been talking about. The Quiet Hour.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the twists were brilliant. I didn't see the ending coming at all, and the second one in the middle? Total shock.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right? That's why I keep recommending it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: But, wow, it's long. Almost three hours. There's a whole subplot with the sister that they could've cut entirely.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'll admit that part dragged. I actually checked my watch twice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same. I might rewatch it sometime, but I'd skip ahead through the sister scenes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's actually a fan edit online that trims about forty minutes. I'll send you the link.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, please do!

questions.stem: What specific part of the movie did the woman think should have been cut?

options.A: The opening sequence of the film
options.B: The final twist near the end
options.C: A subplot involving a sister character
options.D: A long flashback in the middle

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生明确说"姐姐那条副线他们完全可以剪掉"，所以她认为应该删除涉及姐姐这个角色的副线。A错误：对话从未提到开场镜头有问题。B错误：女生其实非常赞美结尾的反转，认为它出人意料。D错误：对话中没有提到中段的闪回情节，这是听起来合理但未提及的细节。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after the conversation?

options.A: Send the woman a link to a shorter fan edit
options.B: Rewatch the movie with the woman this weekend
options.C: Write a negative review about the movie's length
options.D: Stop recommending the movie to other friends

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我会把链接发给你"，指的是网上一个删减了约四十分钟的影迷版本。B错误：对话中没有提到他们要一起重看这部电影，是过度推理。C错误：男生承认有部分拖沓，但他仍然在推荐这部电影，写差评违背他的态度。D错误：他明显仍然欣赏这部电影并继续向他人推荐，与对话信息相反。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, the activities fair ended yesterday and I still can't decide. Should I sign up for the photography club or the intramural soccer team?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, both meet on Tuesdays, right? You can't really do both.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. And I already have my chemistry lab on Thursday nights, so I need to be careful.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, what are you actually hoping to get out of it? Like, friends? Exercise? A new skill?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I want to meet people outside my major. The chem crowd's nice but kind of intense.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then soccer, hands down. The intramural league is super social, and they go out together every weekend.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Even if I'm not great at soccer?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Totally. Half the team had never played before this year. It's all about showing up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'm convinced. I'll register tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to balance lab work with social activities
options.B: Whether photography club fees are worth paying
options.C: The best way to make friends outside one's major
options.D: Which extracurricular activity the woman should join

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：整段对话围绕女生在摄影社和校内足球队之间犹豫不决，男生帮她做选择。A错误：化学实验只是被简单提及作为时间冲突，不是讨论的主题。B错误：对话中完全没有提到会费问题。C错误：交友是她选择活动的动机之一，但这是太狭窄的视角，主题是选哪个活动。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's soccer ability?

options.A: She played competitively in high school
options.B: She has limited experience with the sport
options.C: She refuses to join because of her skill level
options.D: She is the team's most talented new member

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生问"即使我足球踢得不好也可以吗？"暗示她经验有限，所以可以推断她对这项运动经验不多。A错误：她担心自己技术不好，与高中竞技踢球矛盾，与对话信息相反。C错误：男生说不用紧张后她答应报名，她并没有拒绝加入，被否决的选项。D错误：她担心自己不擅长，称她是队里最有天赋的新成员是过度推理。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, didn't you take that weekend pottery class at the community center last month? How was it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I loved it. Way more relaxing than I expected. The instructor was really patient.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking about signing up. What did you actually make?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We started with simple bowls the first weekend, then moved on to mugs. Mine came out a little crooked, but it works.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Aww, that's so cute. Was it expensive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sixty bucks for four sessions, and that includes all the clay and glazes. Pretty good deal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's reasonable. Do I need to bring anything?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just wear old clothes. Trust me, you'll get clay everywhere.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll register this afternoon.

questions.stem: What was the first item the man made in the pottery class?

options.A: A simple bowl
options.B: A crooked coffee mug
options.C: A glazed dinner plate
options.D: A small clay vase

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我们第一个周末从简单的碗开始"，所以他做的第一件东西是简单的碗。B错误：杯子是第二个周末做的，不是第一件，是混淆细节。C错误：对话中没有提到盘子，听起来合理但未提及。D错误：对话中没有提到花瓶，听起来合理但未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do this afternoon?

options.A: Go shopping for new clothes to wear
options.B: Ask the man to attend the class with her
options.C: Sign up for the pottery class herself
options.D: Visit the community center to see the studio

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生在对话最后明确说"我下午就去报名"，因此她下午最可能做的事是给陶艺课报名。A错误：男生让她穿旧衣服，而不是去买新衣服，与男生的建议相反。B错误：对话中没有提到邀请男生一起上课，与对话无关。D错误：她说要去报名，没说要先去社区中心实地参观，是过度推理。

items.situation: a student asking a friend for recommendations on a new couch for the dorm

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, you got that couch for your common room last semester, right? Where did you end up finding it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the secondhand one. I picked it up from that warehouse on Elm Street. They had a bunch of options under three hundred dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Three hundred is still kind of steep for me. My roommates and I are splitting it four ways, so we want to keep it under two hundred total.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: In that case, honestly, just check the student housing forum. People dump furniture at the end of every semester. Last May I saw a leather one going for fifty bucks because the owner was moving out the next day.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, really? Fifty? That's amazing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah. The trick is checking around finals week. Just be ready to pick it up the same day, because they usually want it gone immediately.

questions.stem: According to the conversation, what is the woman's budget constraint?

options.A: Two hundred dollars total, split four ways
options.B: Three hundred dollars for a single buyer
options.C: Fifty dollars maximum from the warehouse
options.D: Whatever the leather couch on the forum costs

questions.explanation: 女生说四人合资，总预算两百以内。B 是男生提到的另一参考价，C 是男生提到的特价例子，D 是混淆。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about successfully finding a cheap couch on the forum?

options.A: Buyers must register with the Elm Street warehouse first
options.B: Buyers must pay the original retail price plus delivery
options.C: Buyers must be ready to pick up the same day they see the listing
options.D: Buyers must wait until the beginning of a semester

questions.explanation: 男生强调要当天搬走，因为卖家急着清空。A 与讲述无关，B 与便宜价格相反，D 时间反了。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So I tried that new place on Birch Street yesterday — Loom Coffee. Have you been?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went once last week. The latte was good, but seven dollars feels like a lot for what's basically a regular drink.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I actually thought the matcha was worth it. It comes with a tiny shortbread cookie and you can sit there with your laptop forever — nobody asks you to leave.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, fair. The seating situation is way better than the cafe by the library. I'll give them that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? And they have those long communal tables with actual outlets. The library cafe charges almost the same but you have to fight for a power strip.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hm. Yeah, maybe I'm judging it on the wrong thing. I was just looking at the drink price by itself.

questions.stem: According to the conversation, what is the man's initial complaint about Loom Coffee?

options.A: The matcha is too sweet for his taste
options.B: The seating is uncomfortable for studying
options.C: The latte is too expensive at seven dollars
options.D: The cookies are stale by the afternoon

questions.explanation: 男生最初的不满是拿铁七元太贵。其他都是错误描述。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's view by the end of the conversation?

options.A: He has decided never to visit Loom Coffee again
options.B: He plans to write a critical review online
options.C: He prefers the library cafe for its quieter atmosphere
options.D: He realizes he was judging the cafe on price alone

questions.explanation: 男生最后承认只看价格判断有偏差。A 与对话相反，B 没提，C 与对比内容相反。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally finished that watercolor class at the community center. Six weeks, every Saturday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, the one you signed up for in February? I was thinking about doing the spring session.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, it was harder than I expected. The instructor was great, but I thought I'd be making pretty landscapes and instead we spent four weeks just on color mixing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wait, four weeks on mixing? That sounds tedious.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It was, at first. But by week five I realized why she made us do it. When we finally did landscapes, I actually knew how to get the colors I wanted. People in my class who skipped the early weeks were just guessing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. That actually makes me less worried about the spring session. I'd rather build a foundation than just produce something that looks okay but I can't replicate.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: The cost of art classes at the community center
options.B: Whether community art classes accept student discounts
options.C: The value of the watercolor class's slow technical foundation
options.D: The instructor's qualifications and previous teaching record

questions.explanation: 对话主要谈这门水彩课慢慢打基础的价值。其他都是分散注意。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's likely decision regarding the spring session?

options.A: He is more inclined to enroll after hearing about the foundation
options.B: He has decided to switch to a different art form entirely
options.C: He is now hesitant because of the time commitment
options.D: He has already paid for the session and cannot cancel

questions.explanation: 男生最后说现在不那么担心，更倾向报名。其他都与对话相反或无关。

items.situation: two students talking about which textbook edition to buy for chemistry

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm trying to decide between the new tenth edition of the chemistry textbook and the older ninth edition. The new one is one-fifty, the old one is twenty bucks used.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: How different can they really be? Chemistry hasn't changed that much.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's what I thought. But Professor Castellano specifically said the practice problems are renumbered in the new edition, and homework references the tenth edition's problem numbers.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. That's a real problem. You'd have to map problems back and forth every week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. My friend tried that last semester for organic chem and gave up by week four. She ended up borrowing the new edition from the library reserves every Sunday night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wait, the library has reserves? That might be the answer. You'd save the money and still have access to the right problem numbers.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Why Professor Castellano changed editions this semester
options.B: Whether organic chemistry has become harder over time
options.C: Why the textbook publisher renumbered the problems
options.D: Whether to buy the new chemistry textbook or use a cheaper alternative

questions.explanation: 对话核心是是否买新书或用便宜方案。其他都是支撑性细节或不相关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's suggestion regarding library reserves?

options.A: He thinks the woman should buy the new edition just in case
options.B: He thinks reserves can substitute for owning the new edition
options.C: He thinks the library has stopped offering reserves this year
options.D: He thinks the older edition is academically superior

questions.explanation: 男生提议用图书馆储备本省钱又有正确题号。其他都是相反或不相关。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before an exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Our linguistics midterm is on Thursday. Want to study together Tuesday night?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Tuesday is tough. I have my phonetics lab until eight. We could meet after, but I'd be pretty fried.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Wednesday is too late — I want at least one full day to review on my own after.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: What about Tuesday morning? I'm free from ten until my lab at two.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that actually works. I was thinking of evening because the library group rooms get crowded, but the morning slot is usually wide open.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. And we can grab the corner room with the whiteboard — that one's perfect for the syntax tree diagrams we'll need to practice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. Tuesday at ten, the room with the whiteboard. I'll bring the practice tree problems.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to do?

options.A: Schedule a joint study session before their linguistics midterm
options.B: Decide whether to attend the phonetics lab on Tuesday
options.C: Plan a long-term study group for the entire semester
options.D: Choose between competing rooms in the linguistics building

questions.explanation: 对话核心是约语言学期中考前的学习时间。其他都是片面或不相关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the corner room with the whiteboard?

options.A: It is normally reserved for graduate students
options.B: It is particularly suited to the diagrams they need to practice
options.C: It is crowded mainly during morning hours
options.D: It can only be booked one day in advance

questions.explanation: 男生说那个角落的白板房间很适合画句法树。其他都是没提到的或与对话相反。

items.situation: two students comparing notes on a novel they both read

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finally finish The Lighthouse Keeper last night?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah. The ending wasn't what I expected at all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I thought the second half lost steam, especially after the storm chapter.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I get that, but I think the slower pace was the point. The keeper was clearly cracking, and you needed to feel it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Maybe I rushed through it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try going back to the journal entries near the end. They totally reframe his choices.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: Their differing reactions to a novel they both read
options.B: Whether to recommend the novel to a friend
options.C: The author's earlier books in the same series
options.D: A film adaptation of The Lighthouse Keeper

questions.explanation: 整段是两人对同一本书不同的反应。A 正确。B、C、D 都是讲座未涉及的别的话题。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a different novel by the same author
options.B: Recommend the book to her study group
options.C: Revisit the journal entries near the end of the book
options.D: Argue with the man about the storm chapter

questions.explanation: 男生建议她回去看 journal entries，女生承认自己读太快，可推出她会回看那些段落。C 正确。A、B 都是未提及；D 与她 "也许我读太快了" 的态度相反。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before an exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, want to study for the chemistry midterm together on Thursday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. The library or the dorm lounge?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I booked Group Room 4 in the library for two hours starting at six.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Do we need to bring practice problems?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I printed out the Chapter 7 set already. Could you bring the whiteboard markers?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I have a fresh pack. See you at six.

questions.stem: Where will the speakers meet to study?

options.A: The dorm lounge on the second floor
options.B: Group Room 4 in the library
options.C: The chemistry building lounge
options.D: A coffee shop just off campus

questions.explanation: 女生明确说 booked Group Room 4 in the library。B 正确。A 是被讨论却被否决的备选；C、D 都未出现。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do before Thursday?

options.A: Print out the Chapter 7 practice problems
options.B: Reserve a separate group study room himself
options.C: Buy a new chemistry textbook for the midterm
options.D: Make sure he has whiteboard markers ready

questions.explanation: 男生说 I have a fresh pack — 即他将带白板笔。D 正确。A 是女生已做；B 房间已订；C 无关。

items.situation: two students talking about a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you been to that new Korean place on Maple Street yet?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Not yet, but I keep hearing about the bibimbap.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the kimchi pancake is what everyone should be ordering.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Pancake? At a Korean restaurant?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's savory, super crispy, and just five dollars at lunch. Better than the bibimbap, in my opinion.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'll order that next time instead. Thanks for the tip.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: The history of Korean cooking in the area
options.B: Why the new restaurant became popular so quickly
options.C: Which dish at a new restaurant is worth ordering
options.D: How much lunch typically costs at the restaurant

questions.explanation: 两人在讨论哪道菜值得点。C 正确。A 偏题；B、D 都只是一处细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on her next visit?

options.A: Order the kimchi pancake instead of the bibimbap
options.B: Bring some friends along to try every dish
options.C: Skip the lunch menu and order from dinner
options.D: Ask the chef directly for the pancake recipe

questions.explanation: 女生说 I'll order that next time instead，that 指 kimchi pancake，instead 指代替 bibimbap。A 正确。其余都未提或夸大推论。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How are you getting to the new humanities building every morning? It's pretty far from the dorms.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I tried biking last week, but the hill on Pierce Street is brutal. Now I just take the campus shuttle.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I always walk, but it takes me about thirty minutes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The shuttle runs every ten minutes and stops right by the dorm entrance. You should try it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, maybe tomorrow. I usually like the exercise though.

questions.stem: Why did the man stop biking to the humanities building?

options.A: He could not find a safe place to lock his bike
options.B: He preferred the social aspect of the campus shuttle
options.C: He damaged his bicycle in a small accident
options.D: He found the hill on Pierce Street too difficult

questions.explanation: 男生原话: the hill on Pierce Street is brutal。D 正确。A、B、C 均未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a new bicycle for her morning commute
options.B: Consider trying the campus shuttle tomorrow
options.C: Move to a dorm closer to the humanities building
options.D: Set her alarm earlier to give herself more time

questions.explanation: 女生说 Hmm, maybe tomorrow — 表示她会考虑明早试试 shuttle。B 正确。A、C、D 都是夸大或无关的下一步。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking or beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We're still on for Saturday, right? Beach or that hiking trail near the lake?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The forecast says rain in the afternoon. Maybe the trail with the covered shelters?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, good call. The shelters mean we can keep going if it starts pouring.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll grab my rain jacket and bring some extra trail snacks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'll handle the car and gas this time. Pick you up at eight.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Let's do it.

questions.stem: Why did the speakers choose the hiking trail over the beach?

options.A: The trail has shelters that protect them from rain
options.B: The trail is much closer to their dormitory
options.C: The beach has been crowded the last few weekends
options.D: The man does not enjoy swimming in the lake

questions.explanation: 男生说选 trail with the covered shelters，女生回 the shelters mean we can keep going if it starts pouring。A 正确。其余均未涉及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before Saturday?

options.A: Buy a new rain jacket for herself
options.B: Cancel the trip if the rain continues
options.C: Make sure the car has enough gas
options.D: Pack a picnic lunch for both of them

questions.explanation: 女生明说 I'll handle the car and gas this time，因此她会确保油充足。C 正确。A 是男生准备 jacket；B 与对话相反；D 未提。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking or beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are we still on for Saturday? I've been looking at the forecast and it's supposed to be perfect.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'm in. But I was thinking, maybe we should do the coast trail instead of Bear Ridge? It's only an hour drive.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The coast trail? I'd love to, but my car is in the shop until Monday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh. Hmm. Well, I can drive. My tank's full anyway.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are you sure? I can chip in for gas. And I'll grab sandwiches from that place by the library you like.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Don't worry about gas, but sandwiches sound great. Should we leave around eight? Parking fills up fast there.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Eight works for me. I'll text you Friday night to confirm. Oh, and should I bring a swimsuit? Last time I forgot mine.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, definitely. The tide pools by the south end are amazing if you have time to wade in. We can hike first, then check those out.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Final arrangements for a weekend trip to the coast
options.B: Whether to repair the woman's car before the weekend
options.C: How to find a parking spot at Bear Ridge
options.D: Comparing trails for an upcoming class assignment

questions.explanation: 对话围绕周末去海岸路线的最终安排：行程改变、谁开车、出发时间、带什么。其他选项要么是次要细节（修车、停车），要么不在对话内（课程任务）。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man?

options.A: He plans to spend most of the day in the tide pools
options.B: He has never been to the coast trail before
options.C: He is willing to cover the driving without compensation
options.D: He prefers Bear Ridge over the coast trail in general

questions.explanation: 男生说"我可以开车，油箱也满了"，并明确拒绝女生分摊油费（Don't worry about gas），说明他愿意自己承担驾车成本。A夸大；B反转（他建议去海岸说明他了解那里）；D相反（是他建议改去海岸）。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, my playlist has felt stale for months. Every pop singer sounds identical now. Got anything different to recommend?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Define different. Like, completely outside pop, or just a less mainstream version of it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Outside, I think. I tried a jazz album last week and actually liked it more than I expected.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then you should try modern Afrobeat. The drumming is incredible, and the vocals layer in a way pop doesn't. Start with the Lagos compilations.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Lagos? Like, from Nigeria?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah. There's a producer called Tunde who's released three this year. Each track has like five instruments going at once but it never feels crowded.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'm sold. Anything else? I need variety.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If you liked the jazz, look into Brazilian bossa nova too. It's mellower but the chord changes will keep your brain busy. I'd start with the older recordings from the sixties — they're sharper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sixties recordings. Got it. I'll send you my reactions tonight.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the conversation?

options.A: To debate which music genre is most technically demanding to perform
options.B: To exchange suggestions that broaden the woman's listening beyond pop
options.C: To plan a joint music project that combines several different genres
options.D: To compare the woman's musical taste with the man's collection of albums

questions.explanation: 女生明确想换口味，男生给出具体推荐（Afrobeat、bossa nova）。A、C、D不是对话的主要目的。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about Brazilian bossa nova?

options.A: Listening to it requires understanding Portuguese lyrics first
options.B: Only recordings from the 2010s have well-mixed audio quality
options.C: It will eventually replace jazz as the dominant lounge style
options.D: Older recordings offer sharper sound than newer ones

questions.explanation: 男生明确说六十年代的旧录音更清晰（sharper）。A未提；B正好相反；C过度推断。

items.situation: two students chatting about a pottery class one just took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally took that beginner pottery class at the community center. It's so much harder than I expected.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice. Centering the clay on the wheel, right? People underestimate it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. My first bowl looked like an oval. I spent the second half just fixing it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's actually normal. Took me three weeks before I could center consistently. Did the instructor show you the wedging trick?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wedging? She showed me how to slap the clay down a few times but I didn't catch a name for it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's wedging. It pushes the air bubbles out. If you skip it your pot can crack when it's fired.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's good to know. The kiln firing is next week and I was getting nervous.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, you'll be fine if you wedge properly. Also, don't make the walls too thin. They look elegant but they warp easily.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether the woman should switch to a different pottery class
options.B: How to repair a cracked bowl after kiln firing has finished
options.C: The woman's experience in her beginner pottery class
options.D: Where to buy supplies for a personal pottery studio

questions.explanation: 对话围绕女生陶艺课经历及男生分享的技巧。A、B、D都没出现。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman before this conversation?

options.A: She did not know the term "wedging" before talking with the man
options.B: She had taken a previous pottery class at the same center
options.C: She intended to drop the class before the kiln firing
options.D: She had been making thin-walled pots on purpose all along

questions.explanation: 女生说她不知道"wedging"这个名字，只看到老师拍打粘土。其他选项无依据。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before an exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are you ready for the macroeconomics midterm on Thursday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really. I'm shaky on the monetary policy chapters. You?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same. I was thinking we should study together. Maybe Wednesday afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wednesday works. Where, though? The main library's been packed all week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: What about the third floor of the economics building? There's that small lounge nobody uses.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh right, the one with the whiteboards. Yeah, perfect for working through diagrams.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Around two? I'll bring my notes from Professor Yamada's review session.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Two is good. I'll bring the practice problems from the textbook. Maybe coffees from the cart downstairs first?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely coffees first. See you Wednesday.

questions.stem: Where do the students decide to meet?

options.A: At the cart downstairs in the main library
options.B: In Professor Yamada's office during her hours
options.C: In the third-floor lounge of the main library
options.D: In the third-floor lounge of the economics building

questions.explanation: 女生提议经济学楼三楼的小休息室，男生同意。A、C混淆了地点细节；B未提。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's exam preparation?

options.A: He has already finished the practice problems by himself
options.B: He has only fully reviewed the monetary policy chapters
options.C: He feels weakest on the monetary policy chapters
options.D: He plans to skip the review session by Professor Yamada

questions.explanation: 男生明确说自己对货币政策章节没把握（shaky on）。A正相反；B反转（不是已经复习好）；D无依据。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How are you getting to campus this semester now that you moved off the main quad?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm biking. It's about fifteen minutes each way. Faster than waiting for the shuttle.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fifteen? That's not bad. Do you bring rain gear for the wet days?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I keep a jacket in my backpack. Worse case, I lock the bike and grab the bus.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. I take the seven A bus. It's reliable but it gets crowded around eight.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the seven A is intense in the morning. What about walking? You're closer than I am.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I tried walking the first two weeks. It took twenty-five minutes and my legs were tired by the time I sat down in class.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right, walking's deceptive. Looks short on the map but the hill near the engineering building is brutal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. The bus is worth it just to skip that hill.

questions.stem: How long does the man's bike commute take each way?

options.A: About fifteen minutes
options.B: Around twenty-five minutes
options.C: Roughly thirty minutes
options.D: Less than ten minutes

questions.explanation: 男生明确说骑车单程大约十五分钟。其他都是混淆的时间数。

questions.stem: Why does the woman choose to take the bus rather than walk?

options.A: She prefers reading on the bus during her commute
options.B: She finds the hill near engineering too tiring to walk
options.C: She lives much farther than the man from campus
options.D: Her schedule prevents her from walking before class

questions.explanation: 女生明确说工程楼附近那段坡很费力，公交是为了避开那段坡。A、D未提；C相反（男生在更远的地方）。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for Saturday's dinner, I was thinking we'd grab some cheese from that little shop on Elm Street.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, good call. What were you thinking? Maybe a brie and a sharp cheddar?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. And maybe a goat cheese too, since Lily said she's lactose sensitive but can handle goat.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nice. I'll grab some crackers, grapes, and a baguette on my way over.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. Don't forget the honey — it's amazing with the cheddar.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Will do. I'll text you a photo of everything before I head to your place.

questions.stem: According to the woman, why is she choosing goat cheese?

options.A: Lily can tolerate goat cheese despite being lactose sensitive.
options.B: It is the cheapest option at the Elm Street shop.
options.C: It pairs best with the bread the man is bringing.
options.D: It is the only cheese the shop has in stock today.

questions.explanation: 女生提到Lily乳糖不耐受但可以接受山羊奶酪。其他选项都没有在对话中出现。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do before going to the dinner?

options.A: Pick up additional cheese from the same shop.
options.B: Call Lily to confirm what she can eat.
options.C: Buy crackers, grapes, and a baguette.
options.D: Skip the dinner because of work obligations.

questions.explanation: 男生明确表示会带饼干、葡萄和法棍。A、B、D都与他的承诺不符。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, how's the new job at the soap factory going? You said you started last week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's actually been okay. The shifts are long, but I make eighteen dollars an hour, which is way better than the cafe paid.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh nice. And it's only on weekends, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, Saturday and Sunday mornings. So I can still take my Tuesday-Thursday classes without any conflict.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. Are you saving the extra income for something specific?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'm trying to put away enough for the summer field study in Ecuador.

questions.stem: According to the man, what is one advantage of his new job?

options.A: The factory is closer to campus than the cafe was.
options.B: It pays significantly more per hour than the cafe paid.
options.C: It offers free training in industrial machinery.
options.D: It provides a fixed Wednesday lunch break daily.

questions.explanation: 男生明确表示新工作的时薪比咖啡店高很多。其他选项都未提及。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's plans for the summer?

options.A: He plans to take additional weekday classes.
options.B: He intends to switch back to working at the cafe.
options.C: He hopes to live in the dorms permanently year-round.
options.D: He hopes to participate in a field study abroad.

questions.explanation: 男生明确说存钱是为了去厄瓜多尔的暑期野外研究。其他选项与对话信息不符。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, so the bus schedule's confusing — the 8:15 express to Riverton runs weekdays only, but the local on Saturday leaves at 8:45.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. The local takes about an hour longer, right?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, about 70 extra minutes. We'd hit Riverton just after 11.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's cutting it close. The lighthouse tour begins at 11:30.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True. There's a regional train at 8:00 — same arrival as the express but a tiny bit more expensive.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Worth it. Let's grab the train. Way less stress.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to decide?

options.A: Whether to skip the lighthouse tour entirely.
options.B: Which lighthouse to visit on the weekend.
options.C: Which form of transportation to take to Riverton.
options.D: Whether to invite additional friends on the trip.

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕选择前往Riverton的交通方式。其他选项偏离话题。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the train option?

options.A: It will allow them to arrive in time for the tour.
options.B: It runs only on weekdays during the season.
options.C: It costs significantly less than the local bus.
options.D: It is the slowest of the three options discussed.

questions.explanation: 男生说火车选项压力小，结合到达时间与游览开始时间，可推断他们能赶上。其他选项与对话内容相反或不符。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before an exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are you free tomorrow afternoon? I really need a study buddy for Thursday's calculus exam.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'm free after 2. The chapter on series is killing me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same. Want to meet at the engineering library? The fourth floor's quieter than the main one.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, can we try Cafe Linden instead? They have whiteboards now.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, perfect for working through proofs.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. Let's do Linden at 2:30 then.

questions.stem: According to the man, why does he suggest Cafe Linden?

options.A: It has shorter lines than the engineering library.
options.B: It opens earlier on weekday mornings.
options.C: It offers free coffee to engineering students.
options.D: It now has whiteboards available for use.

questions.explanation: 男生明确说咖啡店现在有白板。其他选项都未提及。

questions.stem: What will the students most likely do on Wednesday afternoon?

options.A: Visit the engineering library on the fourth floor.
options.B: Meet at Cafe Linden to study together.
options.C: Postpone studying until the day of the exam.
options.D: Form a larger group with additional classmates.

questions.explanation: 对话最后他们约定在Linden咖啡馆见面。其他选项与对话结论不符。

items.situation: two friends recommending a podcast or TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you finished that history podcast I sent you last week?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the one about ancient navigation. It was actually fantastic — I binged the whole series.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? The host explains everything so clearly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. Hey, you should try that documentary series on glassblowing. Each episode is short — only twenty minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Glassblowing? Hmm, I wouldn't have thought of that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me, the cinematography is incredible. You'll be hooked after one episode.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly doing?

options.A: Exchanging recommendations for shows they enjoyed.
options.B: Planning a documentary project for a class assignment.
options.C: Complaining about the price of streaming subscriptions.
options.D: Discussing which professor recommended the podcast.

questions.explanation: 对话围绕互相推荐播客和纪录片展开。其他选项偏离对话焦点。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's reaction to the glassblowing series?

options.A: She has already finished watching the series.
options.B: She refuses to watch any documentary content.
options.C: She is curious and likely to give the series a try.
options.D: She prefers podcasts to visual media in general.

questions.explanation: 女生表示"没想到"但没拒绝，加上男生强调摄影出色，暗示她可能愿意尝试。其他选项与对话不符。

items.situation: a student asking the writing center about thesis tutoring hours

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I want to set up regular tutoring for my senior thesis. Are the hours different this semester?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, actually. We've extended evening hours to nine, but Saturday slots got cut. Thursdays fill up fastest.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. I work Thursday mornings. Can I book recurring weekly sessions?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. You can lock in the same Tuesday or Wednesday slot for the whole term.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Tuesday at five sounds perfect. Do I sign up online?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yep, just use the writing center portal. Pick recurring, and I'll confirm it from this end.

questions.stem: What is the woman mainly trying to do?

options.A: Reschedule a session that conflicts with her Thursday job
options.B: Arrange recurring tutoring meetings for her senior thesis
options.C: Apply for a part-time job at the writing center
options.D: Find out why the Saturday hours have been canceled

questions.explanation: 女生一开口就说要为毕业论文安排长期的固定辅导，所以B是对话的核心目的。A把周四工作这个细节当主话题，属于以偏概全；C完全没在对话里出现；D只是男生顺带提到的一个变动，并不是女生求助的主要原因。

questions.stem: According to the man, how should the woman book her sessions?

options.A: By calling the writing center front desk directly
options.B: By emailing the man her preferred weekly schedule
options.C: By signing a paper clipboard outside the office
options.D: By using the writing center's online booking portal

questions.explanation: 男生明确说"just use the writing center portal"，所以D正确。A、B、C都没有在对话中提到，是与情境相关但未被提及的干扰项。

items.situation: a student checking on a special-ordered textbook at the campus bookstore

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm checking on a special order. The book is called Practical Linguistics.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's listed. Shipped from the publisher Monday — usually about five business days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's cutting it close. My paper is due Saturday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, if you're nervous, the library keeps a reserve copy you can borrow overnight.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. I didn't realize they had reserves on this one. Thanks!

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem. We'll text you when yours arrives either way.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the special-ordered book?

options.A: It typically arrives about five business days after shipping
options.B: It was shipped from a regional warehouse on Friday
options.C: It costs more than the library's reserve copy edition
options.D: It is currently out of stock at the publisher's office

questions.explanation: 男生明确说"Shipped from the publisher Monday — usually about five business days"，所以A是对的。B把发货日期改了（实际是周一，不是周五）；C和D的价格和缺货信息对话里完全没提，属于相关但未陈述/错误细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before Saturday?

options.A: Ask the publisher to expedite her shipment directly
options.B: Cancel her special order at the campus bookstore
options.C: Borrow the library's reserve copy of the book
options.D: Submit her paper without using the book at all

questions.explanation: 女生听到"library keeps a reserve copy"后说"Smart"并道谢，强烈暗示她会去图书馆借这本备用书，所以C正确。A过度推断（没说要联系出版社）；B被她拒绝（she's still keeping the order, will be texted when it arrives）；D完全没依据。

items.situation: two students discussing broken machines in the dorm laundry room

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you try the laundry room last night? Every dryer was broken or jammed.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the basement set has been a nightmare all week. I started using the second-floor machines instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, second floor has machines? I had no idea.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They just installed them in August. Honestly, half the residents still don't know.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are they busy? I always end up waiting forty minutes downstairs.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Almost empty most evenings. Most people stick with what they know.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll switch tonight. Thanks for the tip!

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about the basement laundry room?

options.A: It has been closed for major renovations this week
options.B: Its machines have been malfunctioning all week long
options.C: It now requires an extra coin fee per load to use
options.D: Only graduate residents are allowed to access it

questions.explanation: 男生说"the basement set has been a nightmare all week"，与女生看到的"every dryer was broken or jammed"互相印证，B正确。A把"机器坏了"夸大成"整个房间关闭装修"；C、D额外的收费和资格限制对话里完全没有依据。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Try the laundry machines on the second floor
options.B: Report the broken dryers to the dorm office
options.C: Wait downstairs until the basement set is repaired
options.D: Carry her laundry to an off-campus laundromat

questions.explanation: 女生听完男生介绍"second-floor machines"后说"I'll switch tonight"，所以她最可能晚上去试二楼的机器，A正确。B、D对话里都没有提到的延伸动作；C恰好相反——她明确要切换、不再等地下室。

items.situation: a student asks an advising-office staff member which elective to register for next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to pick an elective for next term, and I'm stuck between Intro to Film and Public Speaking.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Both are popular. What's your major again?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Marketing. I figured Public Speaking might be more useful, but it meets at eight in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, the system shows Public Speaking still has a few open seats. Film's actually waitlisted already.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, waitlisted? Then I guess the choice kind of makes itself.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Pretty much. And honestly, presentation skills will help you a lot in marketing.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about the Intro to Film course?

options.A: It already has a waitlist.
options.B: It meets early in the morning.
options.C: It is not useful for marketing.
options.D: It has several open seats left.

questions.explanation: 男生明确说“Film's actually waitlisted already”，所以电影课已经有等候名单，A 正确。B 把“早上八点上课”这个细节张冠李戴了——那是女生说的 Public Speaking 的情况；C 男生说的是 Public Speaking 对市场营销有帮助，并没说电影课没用；D 与对话相反，有空位的是 Public Speaking，不是电影课。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Drop the elective requirement entirely
options.B: Add her name to the film course waitlist
options.C: Register for the public speaking course
options.D: Switch her major to communications

questions.explanation: 电影课已满且需排队，而 Public Speaking 还有空位、又对市场营销有用，女生也说“the choice kind of makes itself”，因此她最可能报名 Public Speaking，C 正确。A 属于过度推断，她从未想放弃选修；B 是她实际上要避开的选项（排队等候）；D 完全没有依据，对话从未提到换专业。

items.situation: a student asks a bookstore clerk whether to buy a course textbook now or wait for the rental restock

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Excuse me, do you still have the biology textbook for sale? The shelf looks empty.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We sold out of the new copies this morning, but the rental version comes back in stock Friday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Friday? My first lecture is Thursday, though.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The first chapter's posted free online, so you'd be fine for one class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that helps. Is the rental a lot cheaper?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: About half the price. I'd just wait the two days if I were you.

questions.stem: Why is the textbook currently unavailable?

options.A: The store never ordered enough copies.
options.B: The new copies sold out that morning.
options.C: The publisher delayed the latest edition.
options.D: It can only be purchased online now.

questions.explanation: 店员说“We sold out of the new copies this morning”，所以是当天上午新书售罄，B 正确。A 没有依据，店员没说订得不够；C 对话从未提到出版社延迟；D 把“第一章免费在线”误读成整本书只能网购，店员说的是周五租赁版会补货。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Buy a new copy from another bookstore
options.B: Drop the biology course this term
options.C: Skip her Thursday lecture entirely
options.D: Wait until Friday for the rental copy

questions.explanation: 第一章免费在线让她周四的课不受影响，租赁版又便宜一半且周五到货，店员也建议“just wait the two days”，所以她最可能等到周五租书，D 正确。A 没有依据，没提到去别家书店；B 属于过度推断，她从未想退课；C 与对话相反——免费章节正是让她不必缺课。

items.situation: a student talks with a transportation-office clerk about getting a bus pass, a bike permit, or just walking

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just moved into the north dorms and I'm trying to figure out the cheapest way to get to class.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. We offer a semester bus pass, a bike permit, or, if you're close, walking's free of course.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The north dorms are about a twenty-five minute walk, which feels like a lot before an early class.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then the bus pass is probably your best bet. The route from north dorms runs every ten minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: And the bike permit? I do have a bike back home I could bring up.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's cheaper than the bus, but with winter coming, most students up north end up regretting it.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to register a bicycle on campus
options.B: Which dormitory the woman should choose
options.C: The best commuting option for the woman
options.D: Why the campus bus route was changed

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女生“最便宜/最合适的到课方式”——公交、自行车还是步行——展开，因此主旨是为她选择通勤方式，C 正确。A 只是其中一个被讨论的选项（自行车许可），太窄；B 她已经搬进北宿舍，并不在选宿舍；D 对话从未提到线路变更。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about getting the bike permit?

options.A: It may be a poor choice once winter arrives
options.B: It is currently sold out for the semester
options.C: It costs more than the semester bus pass
options.D: It is not available to north dorm residents

questions.explanation: 男生说自行车许可虽更便宜，但“with winter coming, most students up north end up regretting it”，暗示入冬后骑车不是好选择，A 正确。B 没有依据，他没说许可售罄；C 与对话相反，他明确说自行车比公交便宜；D 也不成立，他并未说北宿舍居民不能办许可。

items.situation: a student asks a library staff member about a novel that is checked out, exploring why it is in such high demand

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm looking for the novel Northern Light, but the catalog says all copies are checked out.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Ah, that one. A professor put it on the required reading list for two big literature sections this term.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that explains the wait. So it's not just me who couldn't find it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. We've actually ordered extra copies, but they won't arrive for a couple of weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I just need it for one essay, not the whole term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: In that case, the e-book version has no waitlist at all. You can borrow it instantly.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: A complaint about an inaccurate library catalog
options.B: A disagreement over a professor's reading list
options.C: Why the library is purchasing fewer novels
options.D: A student's difficulty obtaining a popular novel

questions.explanation: 对话核心是女生想借却借不到这本热门小说，馆员解释原因并给出替代方案，因此主旨是她获取这本小说遇到困难，D 正确。A 目录其实是准确的（确实都被借走了），并非投诉目录有误；B 没人对阅读书单有异议；C 与对话相反，图书馆是加订而非减少。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Wait two weeks for the new print copies
options.B: Borrow the e-book version of the novel
options.C: Add her name to the physical hold list
options.D: Ask the professor to lend her a copy

questions.explanation: 女生只为一篇论文短期需要这本书，而电子版“no waitlist at all, borrow it instantly”，因此她最可能借电子版，B 正确。A 是被排除的选项——她明说不需要等整个学期；C 纸质版仍要排队，与她“只用一篇论文”的需求不符，且馆员已提供更快的电子版；D 完全没有依据，对话没提向教授借书。

items.situation: a student asks a dining-services staff member about a meal-plan swipe problem before grabbing lunch

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my meal card just got declined at the register, but I'm sure I still have swipes left.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check. Hmm, the system shows your plan reset this morning, so your swipes are actually fine.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then why won't it scan?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Looks like the magnetic strip on your card is worn out. The reader just can't read it anymore.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. Can I still get lunch right now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. I'll enter your ID number manually today, and you can grab a free replacement card at the ID office.

questions.stem: Why was the woman's meal card declined?

options.A: The card's magnetic strip is worn out.
options.B: Her meal plan ran out of swipes.
options.C: Her plan had not reset for the term.
options.D: She typed in the wrong ID number.

questions.explanation: 店员检查后说“the magnetic strip on your card is worn out. The reader just can't read it anymore”，所以是磁条磨损导致刷不出，A 正确。B 与对话相反，系统显示她的次数其实够用；C 也相反，店员说她的套餐当天早上已经重置；D 没有依据，手动输 ID 是店员之后才做的事，不是被拒原因。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after lunch?

options.A: Add more swipes to her meal plan
options.B: Pay for her lunch with cash instead
options.C: Get a replacement card at the ID office
options.D: Report the broken register to a manager

questions.explanation: 店员明确说她可以去 ID 办公室“grab a free replacement card”，磁条坏了也确实需要换卡，因此她午饭后最可能去办新卡，C 正确。A 没有依据，她的次数本来就够，不需要加；B 是被排除的——店员手动输入 ID 让她不必付现；D 问题出在卡的磁条而非收银机，所以无需报修收银机。

items.situation: a student asks the registrar staff to fix a closed class and gets onto a waitlist

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm trying to register for Statistics 201, but the system keeps saying the section is closed. Is there any way to get in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check. Yeah, that morning section filled up last week. But there's a waitlist, and honestly, spots open up pretty often in the first two weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? So I should just add my name?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. You sign up through the student portal, not here. If a seat opens, you'll get an email and have twenty-four hours to claim it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, twenty-four hours. I'll keep an eye on my inbox then. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the registrar's office?

options.A: To drop a statistics course she dislikes
options.B: To get help registering for a full class
options.C: To complain about a billing error
options.D: To change her major to statistics

questions.explanation: 女士开口就说她想注册Statistics 201但系统显示该section已关闭，问有没有办法进去，所以她是来寻求帮助注册一门已满的课。A错误，她想加入而非退课，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。C错误，对话完全没提到账单问题，是另一个校园话题（wrong_topic）。D错误，她只是想注册一门统计课，并未提及换专业（wrong_topic）。

questions.stem: What does the man say will happen if a seat opens up?

options.A: She will be charged a waitlist fee
options.B: The office will call her phone
options.C: She will receive an email and have 24 hours to claim it
options.D: She must come back to the office in person

questions.explanation: 男士明确说如果有座位空出，她会收到一封邮件，并有二十四小时来确认。C正确。A错误，对话从未提到任何候补费用（related_but_not_stated）。B错误，男士说的是邮件通知，不是打电话，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。D错误，男士特意说候补名单要通过学生门户网站操作，而不是来办公室，与原文相反（misattributed）。

items.situation: a student asks library staff about an overdue book and renewal options

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I think this book is overdue. I totally forgot about it. How much do I owe?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Let me scan it. Hmm, it's two days late, but there's no fine. We dropped late fees on regular loans last semester.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's a relief! I actually still need it for my paper. Can I keep it longer?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure, I can renew it for another two weeks, as long as nobody else has placed a hold on it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great, nobody has, right? Then please renew it. Thanks so much!

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man about the overdue book?

options.A: There is no late fee to pay
options.B: He owes a small fine for two days
options.C: The book must be returned today
options.D: He is banned from borrowing again

questions.explanation: 女士扫描后说虽然晚了两天，但没有罚款，因为上学期取消了常规借阅的滞纳金。A正确。B错误，她明确说没有罚款，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。C错误，她反而同意续借两周，并未要求今天归还，与原文相反（misattributed）。D错误，对话从未提到禁止借书（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Charge the man a renewal fee
options.B: Renew the book for two more weeks
options.C: Place a hold on the book for someone else
options.D: Tell the man to find a different copy

questions.explanation: 男士确认没人预约后请女士续借，女士之前也说只要没人预约就可以续借两周，所以她接下来最可能续借这本书。B正确。A错误，续借没有提到任何费用，属于过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，给别人下预约与对话情境相反，是被否定的情况（rejected_option）。D错误，让他另找一本与对话内容无关（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff to switch to a quieter dorm room

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to request a room change. My current room faces the courtyard, and there's noise from events almost every night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm sorry to hear that. We do have a few openings on the quiet floor in Building C. It's reserved for students who need a low-noise environment.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds perfect. Is there a fee to transfer?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No fee, but you'll need to fill out a transfer form and have your current roommate sign off that you've cleared your things.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. Where do I get the form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the housing portal under 'Room Transfer.' Submit it online, and we'll process it within three business days.

questions.stem: What does the man say the woman must do to transfer?

options.A: Pay a small transfer fee first
options.B: Find a replacement for her old room
options.C: Submit a form with her roommate's sign-off
options.D: Move out within twenty-four hours

questions.explanation: 男士说没有费用，但她需要填一份转宿表，并让现在的室友签字确认她已清空物品，所以正确答案是提交带室友签字的表格。C正确。A错误，男士明确说没有费用，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B错误，对话从未提到要为旧房间找接替者（related_but_not_stated）。D错误，男士说处理需要三个工作日，并未要求二十四小时内搬出（wrong_detail）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Move into Building C right away
options.B: Go online to find and submit the transfer form
options.C: Ask her roommate to switch rooms too
options.D: Complain to the event organizers about the noise

questions.explanation: 男士告诉她表格在住宿门户网站的'Room Transfer'栏目下，在线提交即可，所以她接下来最可能上网找到并提交转宿表。B正确。A错误，转宿需要先提交表格并等三个工作日处理，立刻搬入属于过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，对话只需室友签字确认，没说室友也要换房，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。D错误，她选择的是换房而不是向活动组织者投诉噪音，是被放弃的做法（rejected_option）。

items.situation: a student asks IT staff why campus Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting on her laptop

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my laptop keeps dropping off the campus Wi-Fi every few minutes. It's driving me crazy during lectures.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Are you connected to the network called 'CampusNet' or the one labeled 'CampusNet-Guest'?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Um, the guest one, I think. Does that matter?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the issue. The guest network times out every fifteen minutes by design. You should register your laptop on the secured 'CampusNet' instead, and it'll stay connected.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh! How do I register it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Forget the guest network first, then select 'CampusNet' and log in with your student ID and password. It remembers your device after that.

questions.stem: According to the man, why does the woman's laptop keep disconnecting?

options.A: Her laptop's wireless card is faulty
options.B: She is on the guest network, which times out
options.C: The campus Wi-Fi is down for maintenance
options.D: Her student ID has expired

questions.explanation: 男士指出问题在于她连的是访客网络'CampusNet-Guest'，而该网络按设计每十五分钟超时一次，所以掉线是因为用了会超时的访客网络。B正确。A错误，男士没说她的无线网卡有问题，原因是网络选错了（wrong_detail）。C错误，对话没提到Wi-Fi在维护停机（wrong_topic）。D错误，学生ID会用来登录安全网络，但从未说它过期了（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a new wireless adapter for her laptop
options.B: Keep using the guest network during lectures
options.C: Forget the guest network and log in to CampusNet
options.D: Report the problem to her professor

questions.explanation: 男士告诉她先忘记访客网络，再选'CampusNet'并用学生ID和密码登录，设备就会被记住，所以她接下来最可能这样做。C正确。A错误，问题不在硬件，买新网卡属于过度推断（over_inference）。B错误，继续用会超时的访客网络与建议相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，向教授报告这个技术问题与对话内容无关（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks dining-hall staff about adding more money to a meal plan

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, my meal plan balance is almost empty, and it's only mid-semester. Can I add more money to it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure! You can add 'dining dollars' anytime. A lot of students top up around now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh good. Do I have to pay cash here at the counter?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No, it's easier online. Just log into the dining account page and add funds with a card. It shows up on your ID instantly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect, instant is great. I'll do it before lunch then. Thanks!

questions.stem: How does the woman say the man can add money to his meal plan?

options.A: By paying cash at the dining counter
options.B: By visiting the registrar's office
options.C: By adding funds online with a card
options.D: By waiting until the next semester

questions.explanation: 女士说不用付现金，在线更方便，登录餐饮账户页面用银行卡充值即可，且会立刻显示在ID上，所以正确答案是在线用卡充值。C正确。A错误，她明确说不用在柜台付现金，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B错误，对话没提到去注册办公室，属另一个校园场所（wrong_topic）。D错误，她说随时都能充值，男士也打算午餐前就充，无需等到下学期（wrong_detail）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Add dining dollars online before lunch
options.B: Pay cash at the counter right now
options.C: Cancel his meal plan for the semester
options.D: Ask his roommate to share a meal plan

questions.explanation: 男士听完后说'我午餐前就去弄'，而女士建议的方式是在线充值，所以他接下来最可能在午餐前在线充入餐饮美元。A正确。B错误，女士说不用付现金，男士也选了在线方式，现金支付是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，他想充钱继续用，取消餐饮计划与情境相反，属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到和室友共用餐饮计划，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks an academic advisor whether to drop a heavy course load

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm taking six courses this term, and I'm completely overwhelmed. I'm thinking of dropping one, but I'm worried about falling behind.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six is a lot. Which one are you considering dropping?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Probably the elective, the art history one. It's interesting, but it's the heaviest reading load.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Here's the thing: dropping an elective won't delay your graduation at all, since you've already met your core requirements for the year. You can always retake it later.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's reassuring. So it won't hurt me down the line?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. And the drop deadline is Friday, so you have time to think it over before deciding.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main concern in this conversation?

options.A: She failed an art history exam
options.B: She wants to add a seventh course
options.C: Whether dropping a course will delay her graduation
options.D: She cannot find the art history classroom

questions.explanation: 女士说她修了六门课感到不堪重负，想退掉一门但担心落后、影响进度，顾问也正是围绕'退课不会推迟毕业'来安慰她，所以她主要担心退课是否会推迟毕业。C正确。A错误，对话没提到考试不及格（wrong_topic）。B错误，她想退掉一门而不是再加一门，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。D错误，对话从未涉及找不到教室（wrong_topic）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Drop the art history course immediately
options.B: Take time to decide before Friday's deadline
options.C: Add another elective to her schedule
options.D: Switch her major to art history

questions.explanation: 顾问最后说退课截止日是周五，她还有时间再考虑后再决定，女士也表现出宽慰但未当场决定，所以她最可能在周五截止前花点时间考虑。B正确。A错误，顾问特意说她有时间考虑，立刻退课属过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，她正为课业过重发愁，再加一门选修与情境相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，对话没提到换专业，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks bookstore staff whether to buy or rent a pricey textbook

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Excuse me, this chemistry textbook is almost two hundred dollars. Is there a cheaper option?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You can rent it for the semester for about a third of that price. Most students go that route.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A third? That's a huge difference. Are there any downsides to renting?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Just that you have to return it by the last day of finals, and you can't write in it. If you like keeping your books, buying makes more sense.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, I never reread my chem books anyway. Renting it is.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good choice. Bring it to the counter and I'll set up the rental for you.

questions.stem: What is one downside of renting the textbook, according to the woman?

options.A: It costs more than buying it new
options.B: It cannot be used during finals
options.C: You must return it and cannot write in it
options.D: It is only available as an e-book

questions.explanation: 女士说租书的缺点是必须在期末考试最后一天前归还，而且不能在书上做笔记，所以正确答案是要归还且不能写字。C正确。A错误，租书只要约三分之一的价格，比买便宜，与原文相反（misattributed）。B错误，她说的是期末最后一天前归还，并非期末期间不能用，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到电子书（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Take the book to the counter to rent it
options.B: Buy the textbook at full price
options.C: Look for the book at another store
options.D: Borrow the textbook from a classmate

questions.explanation: 男士说自己反正从不重读化学书，决定租书，女士也让他把书拿到柜台办理租赁，所以他接下来最可能把书拿到柜台租。A正确。B错误，他已决定租而非买，全价购买是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，对话没提到去别的店找，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。D错误，向同学借书也未被提及，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks gym staff how to sign up for a fitness class that looks full

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I want to join the Tuesday spin class, but the sign-up sheet looks full. Is it too late?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The paper sheet is just a backup. We actually take sign-ups through the rec center app now, and a few spots are still open.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't know there was an app. Is it free to join the class?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Totally free with your student membership. Just download the app, search 'Tuesday Spin,' and tap reserve.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Easy enough. I'll grab the app right now. Thanks!

questions.stem: How does the man say the woman should sign up for the spin class?

options.A: By writing her name on the paper sheet
options.B: By paying a class fee at the desk
options.C: Through the rec center app
options.D: By emailing the class instructor

questions.explanation: 男士说纸质签到表只是备用，现在都是通过rec center应用报名，而且还有空位，所以正确答案是通过健身中心的应用报名。C正确。A错误，他说纸质表只是备用，真正报名要用应用，改了关键方式（wrong_detail）。B错误，他说凭学生会员完全免费，并非在前台交费（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到给教练发邮件（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Download the app and reserve a spot
options.B: Add her name to the paper sign-up sheet
options.C: Pay for a gym membership upgrade
options.D: Try a different class on another day

questions.explanation: 女士说'我现在就去下载应用'，而男士的指引是下载应用、搜索'Tuesday Spin'并点预约，所以她接下来最可能下载应用并预约一个名额。A正确。B错误，纸质表只是备用，写名字是被实际放弃的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，课程凭学生会员免费，升级会员属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到换别的日子上别的课，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks health-center staff how to book a flu shot appointment

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to get a flu shot. Do I need an appointment, or can I just walk in?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: For flu shots this week, we're doing walk-ins only, no appointment needed. But the wait can be long in the afternoons.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good to know. So mornings are quieter?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Much quieter, right when we open at nine. Also, bring your insurance card, though the shot itself is free for students.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Free is great. I have a class until ten-thirty, so I'll come right after. See you then.

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man about getting a flu shot this week?

options.A: He must book an appointment in advance
options.B: It is walk-in only, with no appointment needed
options.C: Flu shots are unavailable until next week
options.D: He needs a referral from a doctor first

questions.explanation: 女士说本周流感疫苗只接受walk-in、不需要预约，所以正确答案是无需预约、直接来打。B正确。A错误，她明确说不用预约，与原文相反（misattributed）。C错误，她说本周就在打疫苗，并非下周才有（wrong_detail）。D错误，对话没提到需要医生转诊（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Come in for a walk-in shot after his 10:30 class
options.B: Book an afternoon appointment online
options.C: Wait until next week to get the shot
options.D: Ask his doctor for a flu shot instead

questions.explanation: 男士说他有课到十点半，打算下课后马上过来，而女士说上午刚开门时人少且接受walk-in，所以他最可能在十点半下课后过来walk-in打针。A正确。B错误，本周是walk-in、不用预约，且下午人多，预约下午与建议相反，属被否定的做法（rejected_option）。C错误，他打算今天就来，等到下周属过度推断（over_inference）。D错误，对话没提到去找自己的医生打，属无关推断（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff why a scholarship payment hasn't arrived

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my scholarship was supposed to be applied to my tuition this month, but my account still shows a balance due. Did something go wrong?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me pull up your file. Ah, I see. Your scholarship is approved, but it's on hold because one document is still missing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Missing? I thought I submitted everything.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You're missing the signed enrollment verification. Once we receive that, the funds release within about two business days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, where do I find that form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the financial-aid portal under 'Forms.' Sign it electronically and upload it, and you'll be all set.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the financial-aid office?

options.A: To apply for a brand-new scholarship
options.B: To find out why her scholarship hasn't paid her tuition
options.C: To appeal a rejected scholarship application
options.D: To request a refund of overpaid tuition

questions.explanation: 女士一开口就说奖学金本应这个月抵学费，但账户仍显示有欠款，问是不是出了问题，所以她是来弄清楚奖学金为何还没抵付学费。B正确。A错误，她已有获批的奖学金，不是来申请全新的（wrong_topic）。C错误，男士说奖学金是approved，并未被拒，与情境相反（reversed_situation）。D错误，她账户是欠款而非多付，要求退款与情境相反（reversed_situation）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Sign and upload the verification form online
options.B: Pay the tuition balance herself today
options.C: Reapply for the scholarship from scratch
options.D: Wait two business days and do nothing

questions.explanation: 男士说缺的是签好的enrollment verification表，并指出它在财务资助门户的'Forms'栏目下，电子签名后上传即可，所以她接下来最可能在线签署并上传该表格。A正确。B错误，问题在于奖学金被暂缓而非真欠款，自己掏钱付学费属过度推断（over_inference）。C错误，奖学金已获批，重新申请与情境相反，是被否定的做法（rejected_option）。D错误，资金需要她先交表才会发放，干等什么都不做与男士的指引相反（rejected_option）。

items.situation: a student asks registrar staff to correct a misspelled name on official records before transcripts go out

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just noticed my last name is misspelled on my student record. It says 'Harten' but it should be 'Hartman.' Can you fix that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, we definitely want that corrected before transcripts go out. Do you have a photo ID with the correct spelling?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, here's my driver's license.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll update it in the system right now. It'll take about two business days to fully process across all your records.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Two days? My transcript request is going out this Friday. Will the new spelling show up in time?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It should, but to be safe, I'd hold off on submitting that transcript request until the correction clears. Just come back Thursday and we'll confirm it's done.

questions.stem: What does the man ask the woman to provide?

options.A: A photo ID with the correct spelling
options.B: A copy of her birth certificate
options.C: Her most recent transcript
options.D: A signed correction form

questions.explanation: 男士明确问女士是否有拼写正确的带照片证件（photo ID），女士随后出示了驾照，所以A正确。B出生证明从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。C成绩单是女士要寄出的东西，不是男士索要的证件，属于misattributed。D签字的更正表格对话中没有出现，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before Friday?

options.A: Submit her transcript request immediately
options.B: Get a new driver's license issued
options.C: Return on Thursday to confirm the correction
options.D: File a complaint about the error

questions.explanation: 男士建议女士先别提交成绩单申请，周四再回来确认更正已完成，女士接受了这个安排，所以C正确。A立即提交申请正是男士建议她暂缓的事，属于rejected_option。B重新办驾照毫无依据，她的驾照拼写本来就是对的，属于over_inference。D投诉错误对话里没有任何线索，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: a student asks financial-aid staff why a scholarship disbursement hasn't appeared in her account yet

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my merit scholarship was supposed to post to my account two weeks ago, but it's still not showing up. Tuition is due Friday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me pull up your file. Hmm, okay. It looks like your scholarship is on hold because we're missing one document.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Missing? I'm pretty sure I turned everything in back in August.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You submitted almost everything. But we never received your signed enrollment verification form. Without it, the funds can't release.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. I had no idea. Where do I even get that form?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's on the financial-aid portal under 'Required Documents.' Fill it out, sign it, and bring it back today. Then I can release the funds before the tuition deadline.

questions.stem: Why is the woman's scholarship on hold?

options.A: Her tuition payment was late
options.B: A required form was never submitted
options.C: Her grades dropped below the minimum
options.D: The scholarship budget ran out

questions.explanation: 男士查到奖学金被冻结是因为缺少一份文件——女士没有提交签字的入学验证表（enrollment verification form），所以B正确。A学费晚交是对话中提到的截止日期问题，但不是冻结奖学金的原因，属于misattributed。C成绩下降从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D奖学金预算用完对话里没有任何依据，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Pay her tuition with a personal loan
options.B: Wait for the scholarship to post automatically
options.C: Appeal the scholarship decision
options.D: Download and submit the verification form today

questions.explanation: 男士告诉女士去财务援助门户网站的'Required Documents'下载表格，填好签字今天带回来，女士也接受了，所以D正确。A用个人贷款交学费对话里完全没提，属于unrelated_next_step。B等奖学金自动到账与男士说的'必须先交表才能放款'直接矛盾，属于rejected_option。C申诉奖学金决定毫无依据，问题只是缺一份表而非决定被撤销，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student asks housing staff about a leaking radiator in her dorm room and when it can be repaired

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, the radiator in my room, 214 in Foster Hall, has been leaking water onto the floor all night. There's a puddle now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yikes, that's not good. I'll file an urgent maintenance ticket right away. Did you put a towel down to stop it from spreading?

conversation.speaker: Woman
