questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说他一离开就给大家发消息（I'll message everyone the second I leave here），要通知大家四点前藏好，所以他最可能去给小组发消息。A选项错误，女生说蛋糕由她负责。C选项错误，他们想保密，男生说Daniel完全不知情，不会告诉他。D选项错误，布置和蛋糕都由女生负责。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that detective movie everyone's talking about. The twist at the end completely fooled me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice, so you'd recommend it? I've been on the fence.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Mostly, yeah. My only complaint is that it drags in the middle. It's almost three hours long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? Yikes. That's a big commitment for a weeknight.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. If you go, save it for the weekend when you're not tired.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'll plan to catch it Saturday afternoon then.

questions.stem: What complaint does the woman have about the movie?

options.A: It is too long and drags in the middle.
options.B: The ending twist was easy to predict.
options.C: The detective plot was hard to follow.
options.D: The ticket was too expensive.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生说她唯一的不满是中段拖沓、电影近三个小时长（My only complaint is that it drags in the middle. It's almost three hours long），所以她唯一的不满是电影太长、中段拖沓。B选项错误，她说结局的反转把她完全骗到了，所以反转并不容易猜。C选项错误，对话没有说剧情难懂。D选项错误，对话没有提到票价。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Skip the movie because it is too long.
options.B: Watch the movie on a weeknight after class.
options.C: See the movie on Saturday afternoon.
options.D: Wait for the movie to come out online.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说他打算周六下午去看（I'll plan to catch it Saturday afternoon then），采纳了女生周末看的建议，所以他最可能周六下午去看。A选项错误，他没有放弃，而是打算周末去看。B选项错误，女生建议别在疲惫的工作日晚上看，男生也同意周末看，所以工作日晚上被否决了。D选项错误，对话没有提到等线上版本。

items.situation: two students talking about a pet or houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, I'm watching my roommate's cat this week while she's at the conference. But I think something's wrong with him.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh no. What's he doing?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: He keeps ignoring his food. I've filled the bowl twice and he barely touches it. He just sits by the window.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. Did your roommate leave instructions?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: She left a note, but I can't read her handwriting. It might say something about wet food.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Why don't you just text her a photo of the note? She can tell you exactly what to do.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a good call. I'll send it right now.

questions.stem: What problem is the woman having with the cat?

options.A: The cat is not eating its food.
options.B: The cat scratched the furniture.
options.C: The cat ran away from the apartment.
options.D: The cat is afraid of the window.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生明确说猫一直不碰食物（keeps ignoring his food, barely touches it），这是她担心的核心问题。B选项错误，对话完全没有提到抓家具。C选项错误，猫并没有跑掉，它就坐在窗边。D选项错误，猫坐在窗边并不是害怕窗户，只是没吃东西，这是对细节的曲解。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Take the cat to a veterinarian immediately.
options.B: Buy a different brand of cat food.
options.C: Send her roommate a photo of the note.
options.D: Wait until her roommate comes home.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生建议把字条拍照发给室友，女生回答"that's a good call. I'll send it right now"，所以她接下来会发字条照片。A选项过度推断，对话中没人提到去看兽医。B选项错误，换猫粮只是男生的猜测，女生并没有决定这么做。D选项错误，女生急于现在就解决，并没有说要等室友回来。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for Saturday's trip to the coast, I checked and there's a train at eight and another at ten. Which should we take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The eight o'clock one. The ten gets us there too late to do anything.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. Oh wait, the eight only runs on weekdays. On Saturdays the first one is actually nine fifteen.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Seriously? That's annoying. Well, nine fifteen still beats ten.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. And return trains leave every hour until eight, so we don't have to rush back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Let's book the nine fifteen. I'll grab the tickets online so we don't have to line up.

questions.stem: Which train will the friends take on Saturday?

options.A: The eight o'clock train.
options.B: The nine fifteen train.
options.C: The ten o'clock train.
options.D: The last train of the day.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生指出周六最早的车是九点十五（first one is actually nine fifteen），男生说"Let's book the nine fifteen"，所以他们坐九点十五的车。A选项错误，八点的车只在工作日开，周六没有。C选项错误，男生明确说十点太晚了。D选项错误，最后一班是傍晚的返程车，不是他们出发要坐的车，这是对信息的张冠李戴。

questions.stem: What does the man imply he will do?

options.A: Drive to the coast instead of taking the train.
options.B: Wait to buy tickets at the station on Saturday.
options.C: Cancel the trip because of the schedule change.
options.D: Purchase the tickets ahead of time online.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说"I'll grab the tickets online so we don't have to line up"，暗示他会提前在网上买票。A选项无依据，全程没有提到开车。B选项与原文相反，他正是为了不排队才提前网购。C选项过度推断，时刻表变了但两人照常出行，没人提取消。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Fifth Street? Everyone's posting about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went yesterday. The coffee's amazing, honestly. But a latte is six dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow. That's almost double the place by campus.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. The drinks are great, but I can't afford it more than once in a while.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Maybe it's a treat-yourself kind of spot, not an everyday thing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a good way to put it. I'd go again for a special occasion, just not on a regular Tuesday.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the new cafe?

options.A: The coffee tastes terrible.
options.B: It is always too crowded to sit.
options.C: The coffee is good but expensive.
options.D: It is closer than the campus cafe.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说咖啡"amazing"但一杯拿铁要六美元（好喝但贵），这正是他的评价。A选项与原文相反，他说咖啡很棒。B选项无依据，对话没提拥挤或没座位。D选项错误，女生说这家几乎是校园那家价格的两倍，而且没有比较距离，是对细节的曲解。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the man will use the cafe?

options.A: He will go there only on special occasions.
options.B: He will go there every morning before class.
options.C: He will stop drinking coffee altogether.
options.D: He will ask the cafe to lower its prices.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生说"I'd go again for a special occasion, just not on a regular Tuesday"，暗示他只会偶尔在特殊场合去。B选项与原文相反，他明确说不会平常日子常去。C选项过度推断，他只是少去这家贵店，没说要戒咖啡。D选项无依据，没人提让店家降价。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I heard you started that part-time job at the packaging factory. How's it going so far?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's okay, but the schedule's killing me. My shift starts at five in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Five? Yikes. How do you even make your nine o'clock lecture?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the thing, I barely do. I'm exhausted by the time class starts, and I keep nodding off.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That doesn't sound sustainable. Can you switch to a later shift?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Maybe. My supervisor mentioned there might be afternoon openings next month. I should probably ask about it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Your grades matter more than a few extra early shifts.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: How to find a part-time job near campus.
options.B: Why the man decided to quit his job.
options.C: Which lecture the man finds most difficult.
options.D: The problem the man's work schedule is causing.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：对话核心是男生五点的早班让他疲惫、上课打瞌睡，影响学习，这是贯穿始终的问题。A选项错误，男生已经有工作了，不是在找工作。B选项与情况相反，他没决定辞职，而是考虑换班次。C选项太狭窄，打瞌睡只是问题的一个表现，且并非某门课特别难。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Drop his nine o'clock lecture.
options.B: Ask his supervisor about an afternoon shift.
options.C: Quit the factory job right away.
options.D: Start going to bed much earlier each night.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说主管提到下个月可能有下午的空缺，"I should probably ask about it"，所以他很可能去问换下午班。A选项过度推断，他想解决的是上课打瞌睡，没说要退课。C选项与原文相反，他考虑的是换班次而非立刻辞职。D选项无依据，对话里没提早点睡这个方案。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Saturday's dinner party, I'm thinking we do a cheese board to start. Maybe three kinds?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. Just remember Priya's coming, and she can't do anything too strong-smelling. The blue cheese last time was a disaster.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh right, I forgot. So let's skip the blue. A soft brie, a sharp cheddar, and something milder?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. And crackers, obviously. I'll handle those and grab some grapes too.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. I'll get the cheeses on my way home, since the good shop is right by my bus stop.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Deal. Oh, and don't forget honey for the brie. Trust me, it makes a difference.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to decide?

options.A: What food to prepare for their dinner party.
options.B: Whether to invite Priya to the party.
options.C: Which grocery store has the lowest prices.
options.D: How to make a blue cheese dish for guests.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：整段对话都在商量晚宴前的食物——选哪几种奶酪、配饼干、葡萄和蜂蜜，这是主旨。B选项错误，Priya已经确定要来，他们只是考虑她的口味，不是讨论是否邀请。C选项无依据，没有比较哪家店便宜。D选项与情况相反，他们专门决定不做蓝纹奶酪，因为上次很糟糕。

questions.stem: Why will the speakers avoid blue cheese this time?

options.A: It is the most expensive cheese available.
options.B: The shop near the bus stop is out of it.
options.C: One guest dislikes strong-smelling foods.
options.D: Neither of them knows how to serve it.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生提到Priya受不了气味太重的东西，上次的蓝纹奶酪是"a disaster"，可推断他们为了照顾这位客人而避开蓝纹奶酪。A选项无依据，对话没提价格。B选项无依据，店里是否有货从未提及。D选项错误，他们不做蓝纹是因为客人口味，不是不会摆盘，属于编造的理由。
